items.title: Microsoft is working on a fix to downgraded GPU drivers in Windows Update — new system uses multiple IDs
items.description: Microsoft has finally confirmed that Windows Update downgrades GPU drivers in certain circumstances. A partial fix is coming later this year to reduce the chances of driver downgrades occurring on newer devices.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 2 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Microsoft is finally looking into fixing automatic GPU driver downgrades on Windows 11 that have plagued users since the OS's launch in 2021. Windows Latest reports that Microsoft has finally acknowledged the issue and is preparing a partial fix that will be released by Q4 of 2026.Specifically, Microsoft is looking to start applying this fix to Windows 11 PCs in April 2026. Only by Q4 will the update be applied to everyone.The fix Microsoft is cooking up narrows down how many devices Windows Update can target with specific GPU driver updates. GPU drivers that are published to the Windows Update catalog using this system incorporate a two-part hardware ID (HWID) in conjunction with computer hardware IDs (CHIDs). The latter is an ID designation designed to identify a specific PC model or hardware configuration.Latest Videos FromThis two-pronged approach gives Windows Update better information on when to expose GPU drivers to Windows 11 machines. The outgoing system Microsoft is using only considers a four-part hardware ID as part of a ranking system. The worst part about this system is that it will not take into consideration the GPU driver model specifically, which is what allows Windows Update to downgrade GPU drivers in the first place. Whichever driver is ranked highest in the Windows Update catalog for systems with a specified hardware ID, Windows Update will push even if the driver itself is outdated. However, there is a caveat with this system — only device display drivers that target new devices can get the update. Existing drivers in the Windows Update catalog may still get forcibly applied on older systems.Windows Update has long had a problem where the service will automatically downgrade GPU drivers in the right circumstances. This is specifically problematic with OEM machines when the OEM has uploaded a GPU driver to the Windows Update catalog. One issue I've run into personally is the inability to clean install Intel Xe drivers on my Windows 11 laptop. Doing so automatically triggers Windows Update to install several older Intel graphics driver revisions.Unfortunately, Microsoft's update won't fix the issue entirely, but it is at least a start. To clarify, this specific fix is different from the Cloud-Initiated Driver Recovery feature Microsoft rolled out recently.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
items.author: Aaron Klotz
items.titleZh: 微软正在修复Windows Update中GPU驱动被降级的问题——新系统采用多个ID
items.descriptionZh:
微软终于着手修复Windows 11中自动降级GPU驱动程序的问题，这一顽疾自2021年系统发布以来一直困扰着众多用户。据Windows Latest报道，微软已正式承认该问题，并准备在2026年第四季度前推出一项部分修复方案。具体时间表为：2026年4月起开始向部分Windows 11 PC应用修复，到第四季度全面推送至所有用户。

### 背景与上下文

Windows Update长期存在一个机制：在特定条件下，它会自动将GPU驱动降级到较旧版本。这一问题在OEM（原始设备制造商）机型上尤为突出，因为OEM厂商可能会向Windows Update目录上传自己的GPU驱动。例如，作者本人就曾在Windows 11笔记本上遇到无法全新安装Intel Xe显卡驱动的问题——一旦手动安装新版驱动，Windows Update便会自动推送并安装多个旧版Intel图形驱动修订版，导致用户被迫使用过时驱动，甚至引发性能下降或兼容性问题。自Windows 11 2021年发布以来，社区反馈不断，但微软直到近期才正式回应。

### 核心技术原理与创新点

当前Windows Update用于判断是否推送GPU驱动的系统仅依赖一个四部分的硬件ID（Hardware ID, HWID）作为排名依据。具体来说，Windows Update目录中为特定硬件ID维护一个驱动排名列表，排名最高的驱动会被强制推送，而该排名并不考虑GPU驱动模型的具体版本。这意味着，即使某款驱动在功能上已经过时或存在缺陷，只要它在目录中针对该硬件ID的排名最高，Windows Update仍会将其推送给用户——这直接导致了驱动被无端降级。

微软计划中的修复方案引入了更精细的识别机制：采用两段式硬件ID（two-part HWID）与计算机硬件ID（Computer Hardware IDs，CHIDs）相结合。CHIDs是一种专门用于标识特定PC型号或硬件配置的ID。新系统通过HWID（识别设备类别）和CHID（识别具体机型或配置）的双重匹配，为Windows Update提供更准确的驱动暴露时机判断。简而言之，只有当GPU驱动在目录中同时匹配了正确的设备型号和具体硬件配置时，Windows Update才会将其视为适用版本，从而避免向不兼容或较旧机型推送过时驱动。

创新点在于：旧系统“一刀切”地按全局排名推送，新系统则引入“型号+配置”的双因子约束，大幅缩小了Windows Update可能错误推送的驱动范围。不过，微软明确表示，这一修复仅适用于新发布到Windows Update目录的设备显示驱动，即针对全新设备的驱动更新。对于已存在于目录中的旧驱动，仍可能被强制应用到较旧的系统上——这意味着问题并未彻底解决，只是初步遏制了最严重的情况。

### 性能参数与对比数据

从技术视角看，新旧系统的核心差异在于匹配精度：旧系统使用单一的四部分HWID（例如：VEN_xxxx&DEV_xxxx&SUBSYS_xxxx&REV_xx），而新系统使用两部分的HWID（例如：VEN_xxxx&DEV_xxxx）加上CHID（例如：OEM品牌+型号+BIOS版本等）。虽然原文未提供具体的对比测试数据，但可以推断，新系统将减少由Windows Update引发的非必要驱动降级案例。例如，同一款GPU芯片（如Intel Xe Graphics）在不同品牌、不同型号的笔记本上，由于OEM定制的散热、功耗、显示器配置不同，所需驱动版本可能不同。新系统能根据CHID区分这些配置，避免将为A品牌设计的驱动错误推送给B品牌。

微软强调，此项修复与近期推出的“云端驱动的驱动恢复”（Cloud-Initiated Driver Recovery）功能不同。后者侧重于驱动崩溃后的自动恢复，而前者关注的是驱动部署前的精准匹配，属于预防性改进。

### 技术影响与应用场景

- **用户层面**：最直接的受益者是Windows 11上的OEM电脑用户，尤其是笔记本用户。他们无需再手动禁用Windows Update的驱动更新，也不必担心安装新版显卡驱动（如Intel Arc、NVIDIA或AMD最新驱动）后瞬间被旧版覆盖。这对于需要最新驱动来获得游戏性能优化、AI计算加速或专业图形渲染支持的用户至关重要。

- **开发与厂商层面**：GPU驱动供应商（如Intel、NVIDIA、AMD）和OEM厂商在向Windows Update目录提交驱动时，需要遵循新的HWID+CHID格式。这意味着驱动发布流程将更规范，厂商需明确标注每个驱动适用的具体机型配置，减少误匹配。

- **局限场景**：由于修复仅针对新驱动，已存储在目录中的历史驱动仍可能触发降级。对于持有老型号设备的用户，问题可能持续存在，除非OEM或GPU厂商主动更新这些旧驱动的元数据。此外，修复的时间点（2026年4月开始）对当前用户而言无异于画饼，微软的漫长工期可能让用户继续寻求第三方工具（如wushowhide.diagcab）或组策略来手动阻止驱动更新。

总之，微软的此次行动虽然只是“部分修复”且落地周期长达数年，但标志着官方对Windows Update驱动管理逻辑的根本性反思——从“全局排名推送”转向“机型级精细匹配”，为未来彻底根除驱动降级问题奠定了基础。对于重度依赖最新GPU驱动的用户，仍需保持警惕并持续关注后续更新。

items.title: Use this map to find the data centers in your backyard
items.description:
An interactive map tracking data center construction and AI policy, built by Isabelle Reksopuro.	

When Oregon resident Isabelle Reksopuro heard Google was gobbling up public land to fuel its data centers in her home state, she didn't initially know what to believe. "There's a lot of misinformation about data centers," she said. "Google has denied taking that land."

Read the full story at The Verge.
items.content: When Oregon resident Isabelle Reksopuro heard Google was gobbling up public land to fuel its data centers in her home state, she didn’t initially know what to believe. “There’s a lot of misinformation about data centers,” she said. “Google has denied taking that land.”Technically, she explains, The Dalles, a city near the Washington state border, sought to reclaim that land, “and Google is just a big, unnamed power user.” The city had in fact asked for ownership of a 150-acre portion of Mount Hood National Forest, claiming it needs access to Mount Hood’s watershed to meet municipal needs as its population — 16,010 as of the 2020 census — grows. But critics, including environmentalists, say the city is trying to secure more water for Google, which has a sprawling data center campus in The Dalles that already consumes about one-third of the city’s water supply.This controversy made Reksopuro curious about the backlash to data centers being built in other communities. So Reksopuro, a student at the University of Washington who studies the connections between tech and public policy, decided to map it out. Using information collected by Epoch AI and data scraped from legislation on data centers, she built an interactive map tracking AI policy around the world. She designed it to be simple enough for anyone to use. “I wanted it to be something that my younger sisters could play through and explore to understand what are the data centers in the area and what’s actually being done about it,” Reksopuro said. She hoped to shift their opinions that way, “instead of like, through TikTok.”Using Claude, the map searches for new sources four times a day and checks them against the existing database Reksopuro built out. “Once it does that, it will write a new summary, add it to the news feed, and populate it on the sidebar,” she said. “I wanted it to be self-updating, since I’m also a student.”Opposition to data centers is one of the few things that unites Americans across party lines. After the initial construction phase, data centers bring few permanent jobs, and send “power costs to records in much of the US,” reports Bloomberg. Though data centers are increasingly controversial, Reksopuro’s map shows that the public response to them is nowhere near universal, nor are the policies governing their construction and use. While there’s been a huge backlash to data centers in Maine — which in April passed the first state-level moratorium on hyperscale data centers, though it was later vetoed by Gov. Janet Mills — Texas “is a huge fan of data centers,” Reksopuro said. “Texas actually passed a tax exemption for data centers.” The state gives data centers more than $1 billion in tax breaks each year, The Texas Tribune reports.Reksopuro isn’t against data centers, but she thinks tech giants benefit from a lack of transparency around data center policies. “Right now, it’s this really opaque thing — and all of a sudden, there’s a facility,” she said. “I think that if people knew about data centers beforehand, it would give them leverage. They would be able to negotiate: ask for job training programs, tax revenue, environmental monitoring, things to improve their community.”
items.author: Gaby Del Valle
items.titleZh: 使用这张地图查找你家附近的数据中心
items.descriptionZh:
俄勒冈州居民Isabelle Reksopuro最初听闻谷歌在她家乡大肆征用公共土地建设数据中心时，并不确定该相信什么。“关于数据中心有很多错误信息，”她说，“谷歌否认征用了那块土地。”她解释说，实际情况是靠近华盛顿州边界的达尔斯市试图收回那片土地，“而谷歌只是一个庞大而无名的用电大户”。该市曾要求获得胡德山国家森林中150英亩土地的所有权，声称随着人口（2020年人口普查为16,010人）增长，需要利用胡德山的水源来满足市政需求。但包括环保人士在内的批评者认为，该市实际上是在为谷歌争取更多水资源——谷歌在达尔斯市拥有一个庞大的数据中心园区，已经消耗了该市约三分之一的供水。

这一争议促使Reksopuro好奇其他社区对数据中心建设的反对声浪。作为华盛顿大学研究科技与公共政策联系的学生，她决定将这一切可视化。利用Epoch AI收集的数据以及从数据中心相关立法中抓取的信息，她构建了一个追踪全球AI政策的交互式地图。她将设计目标定为足够简单，任何人都能使用。“我希望它让我的妹妹们也能玩着探索，了解所在地区有哪些数据中心，以及实际上正在采取什么措施，”Reksopuro说。她希望以此改变她们的看法，“而不是通过TikTok”。该地图借助Claude，每天四次搜索新来源，并与Reksopuro构建的现有数据库进行比对。“一旦完成，它会生成新的摘要，添加到新闻流中，并显示在侧边栏，”她说，“我希望它能自动更新，因为我也还是个学生。”

对数据中心的反对是少数能跨越党派界限、团结美国民众的事情之一。据彭博社报道，在初始建设阶段过后，数据中心带来的永久就业岗位极少，却“推高了美国大部分地区的电力成本，使其达到创纪录水平”。尽管数据中心日益引发争议，但Reksopuro的地图显示，公众对它们的态度远非一致，管理其建设和使用的政策也各不相同。缅因州对数据中心强烈反对——今年4月通过了全美首个州级超大规模数据中心建设暂停令（尽管后来被州长Janet Mills否决），而得克萨斯州“却是数据中心的超级粉丝”，Reksopuro说，“得州实际上通过了数据中心免税政策”。据《得克萨斯论坛报》报道，该州每年为数据中心提供超过10亿美元的税收减免。

Reksopuro并非反对数据中心，但她认为科技巨头从数据中心政策的不透明中获益。“目前这是一个非常不透明的事情——然后突然间，一座设施就冒出来了，”她说，“我认为如果人们事先了解数据中心，他们就会拥有谈判筹码。他们能够进行协商：要求职业培训项目、税收收入、环境监测，以及其他能够改善社区的措施。”

items.title: Live updates from Elon Musk and Sam Altman’s court battle over the future of OpenAI
items.description:
Sam Altman and Elon Musk are facing off in a high-stakes trial that could alter the future of OpenAI and its most well-known product, ChatGPT. In 2024, Musk filed a lawsuit accusing OpenAI of abandoning its founding mission of developing AI to benefit humanity and shifting focus to boosting profits instead. 
Elon Musk, his financial manager and Neuralink CEO, Jared Birchall, and OpenAI cofounder Greg Brockman have already testified before the jury. Shivon Zilis, a former OpenAI board member who shares four children with Musk, took the stand last week, and the courtroom also watched former OpenAI CTO Mura Murati’s videotaped deposition.
For the trial’s third week, Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella appeared on Monday, followed by OpenAI cofounder and former chief scientist Ilya Sutskever. Altman took the stand on Tuesday to refute the Musk argument that he is a liar and a snake, and now on Thursday, May 14th, we will hear the closing arguments, available via an audio live stream on YouTube.
Musk was a cofounder of OpenAI and claims that Altman and Brockman tricked him into giving the company money, only to turn their backs on their original goal. However, OpenAI says that “This lawsuit has always been a baseless and jealous bid to derail a competitor” in a bid to boost Musk’s own SpaceX / xAI / X companies that have launched Grok as a competitor to ChatGPT.
In his lawsuit, Musk is asking for the removal of Altman and Brockman, and for OpenAI to stop operating as a public benefit corporation. Musk has also demanded that OpenAI’s nonprofit receive up to $150 billion in damages he’s asking for if he wins the case.
People to Know
Plaintiff
Elon Musk — plaintiff, OpenAI cofounder and now CEO of rival xAI
Steven Molo — lead counsel for the plaintiff
Jared Birchall — manager of Musk’s family office
Shivon Zilis — former OpenAI board member who shares multiple children with Musk
Defendant
Sam Altman — defendant, CEO of OpenAI 
William Savitt — lead counsel for the defendant
Greg Brockman — president of OpenAI as well as a cofounder 
Ilya Sutskever — former chief scientist at OpenAI and a cofounder
Judge
Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers — aka YGR, trial judge
Here’s all the latest on the trial between Musk and Altman:
Tesla AI is evidence of Musk’s failure, Eddy says.
			
“The documents tell the truth here,” Eddy says.
			
Eddy suggests that Musk’s donated Teslas were, effectively, bribes.
			
Eddy is, wisely, leaning hard on chronology in explaining their defense.
			
Sarah Eddy is giving the closing argument for OpenAI.
			
Molo is done presenting Musk’s closing argument.
			
For all of the very irritating testimony about “the blip,” Molo hasn’t convincingly connected it to his case.
			
Molo is now attempting to make a case against Microsoft.
			
During our break, the jurors were out of the room, and the lawyers were beefing again.
			
We are back on the millions and billions of OpenAI equity that employees have interest in.
			
This is kind of choppy.
			
Molo is now suggesting that the “crown jewels” of OpenAI are its IP.
			
Musk’s early money meant “a great, great deal,” Molo says. Inarguable!
			
Molo calls Shivon Zilis, mother of Musk’s children, “the most even-keeled, even-tempered witness at the trial.”
			
Ah we are back at the museum store funding the museum.
			
Perhaps Mr. Molo is unfamiliar with xAI.
			
Fair enough, Mr. Molo.
			
I see why Molo is leaning on the spoken testimony.
			
Molo is now calling out Altman’s testimony.
			
Molo is suggesting that Greg Brockman’s conduct makes him untrustworthy.
			
Molo has begun his closing statement for Elon Musk, who is in China.
			
YGR is now reading aloud the instructions to the jury.
			
The monitor has left the courtroom.
			
We are now having a fight about a new, large monitor that has appeared on the Musk table.
			
“They didn’t give us page numbers, your honor.”
			
Musk left the country with President Trump despite a judge’s orders.
			
Microsoft and OpenAI rest.
			
In the most boring expert testimony yet, Louis Dudney, a forensic accountant, testified about how those funds were spent.
			
The shade we are getting in here is incredible.
			
The cross is focusing on Coates’ pay.
			
John Coates, OpenAI’s expert witness, is running a demolition derby on Musk’s expert witness.
			
Museum gift shop metaphor found dead in a ditch.
			
We’re listening to an expert witness, David Hemel, a law professor at NYU.
			
During Elon Musk’s all-hands Q&A before departing OpenAI, Achiam said he felt Musk wanted to “race towards AGI.”
			
Achiam is running circles around this lawyer on cross, without doing the annoying things other witnesses have done.
			
Okay, it’s time for the cross of Achiam.
			
“I think he was just upset that he had been challenged,” Achiam said. “This was not friendly.”
			
During the all-hands, Musk expressed concerns about what would happen if DeepMind got to AGI first,
			
“It was a bit like seeing Bigfoot through Plexiglass,” Achiam says of seeing Elon Musk in the office.
			
Ilya Sutskever would get up on tables to give speeches in the early days of OpenAI.
			
Achiam talked about the roles of Greg Brockman and Ilya Sutskever in OpenAI’s early days.
			
Josh Achiam described what it was like to work at OpenAI in 2017.
			
Achiam started at OpenAI as an intern in the summer of 2017, and became a full-time employee in December.
			
Hi my name is Josh Achiam and welcome to “will we see the jackass trophy?”
			
Fairly stupid choice by Musk’s lawyers to go after Microsoft’s major decision rights.
			
Musk cross. I guess we are now going to have a fight about due diligence.
			
“Our due diligence found no conditions related to Elon Musk,” Wetter says.
			
Mike Wetter for Microsoft is taking the stand now.
			
Scott, who is wearing sneakers and a black crew neck under his blazer, seems quite pleasant on cross.
			
We are now getting cross-examination from Musk’s lawyer.
			
Microsoft’s CTO Kevin Scott is on the stand.
			
Microsoft doesn’t want any of this
			
In his testimony, Musk said he never called anyone a jackass.
			
Incredible evidence dispute this morning.
			
Sam Altman was winning on the stand, but it might not be enough
			
About 200 people work on safety at OpenAI.
			
The chair of OpenAI’s safety and security committee said they’ve formally delayed its model releases.
			
Irritatingly, no one has asked him why he’s called “Zico.”
			
Microsoft establishes that OpenAI has other investors…
			
We see the Musk “bait and switch” texts again.
			
What if we had a drinking game for this trial?
			
Musk says, “This is a bait and switch” in a October 2022 text chain.
			
Molo is not doing especially impressive lawyering here.
			
Molo asked Altman if he’d ever fire himself as CEO of the OpenAI for-profit.
			
“The blip” again.
			
Well, I do love a long inquiry into the linear nature of time.
			
The difference between Musk and Altman on cross is really stark.
			
Ronan Farrow’s article is brought up.
			
This cross is spicy!
			
Mr. Molo is going directly in at Altman: “Do you always tell the truth?”
			
“If I knew how difficult and painful this was going to be, I never would have tried,” Altman said.
			
We are now talking about Altman’s investments.
			
“I had poured the last years of my life, and I was watching it be destroyed,” Altman said.
			
“I was in this like fog of war, I didn’t know what was going on,” Altman says of what happened next.
			
We are now onto “the Blip.”
			
Sam Altman says Elon Musk’s mind games were damaging OpenAI
			
OpenAI has raised “approximately $175 billion” in investment, Altman says.
			
Altman seems to be getting into his testimony…
			
Musk didn’t invest in the OpenAI for-profit because “he was no longer going to invest in any startups he did not control.”
			
It looks like Sam Altman discussed the for-profit OpenAI with Elon Musk in detail.
			
“Unlike a lot of other meetings with Mr. Musk, this was a good vibes meeting.”
			
Now into Shivon Zilis. Altman says he retained her on the board to try to keep friendly relations with Musk.
			
“I was annoyed” when Elon Musk tried to recruit talent from OpenAI, Altman said.
			
Musk resigned because he had lost confidence in OpenAI “and did not believe we were going to be successful.”
			
Musk suspended his quarterly donations in 2017. That left OpenAI in “a very tough position.”
			
When it was time to get more capital, Musk was pushing OpenAI to be acquired by Tesla.
			
A particularly “hair-raising moment” for Altman was a succession plan from Musk.
			
Elon Musk has control issues, Altman says.
			
Sam Altman takes the stand in trial against Elon Musk
			
OpenAI has called Sam Altman as a witness.
			
Taylor says the reason OpenAI Foundation has been able to do more work is the recapitalization.
			
Bret Taylor is back on the stand.
			
We’ve talked about how this case isn’t just for whatever happens in the court…
			
Bret Taylor has been asked to slow down twice.
			
“OpenAI is decidedly not profitable,” Taylor said.
			
There’s “a lot of tension” between LLMs and what Taylor calls “content companies”…
			
Plantiff rests. OpenAI calls its first witness, Bret Taylor of OpenAI Foundation.
			
Ilya Sutskever says he was uncomfortable with Musk’s large ownership demand.
			
Sutskever’s testimony is kind of a snooze so far.
			
Satya Nadella is excused.
			
A lot of people contact Satya Nadella about their boards, apparently!
			
Microsoft’s lawyer is now back with Nadella.
			
We are discovering that Satya Nadella knows very little about the OpenAI nonprofit.
			
I can’t speak for the jury but I am very, very sick of hearing about “the blip.”
			
“Not consistently candid” press release about Sam Altman’s firing is what Molo is citing as why Nadella should have known why Altman was fired.
			
We are arguing now about risk and return.
			
“I don’t want to be IBM and OpenAI to be Microsoft.”
			
We are on cross, with Steven Molo for Musk.
			
Satya Nadella seemed to forget he currently served on the board of a nonprofit.
			
What is Copilot?
			
During Altman’s ouster, Satya Nadella tried to reassure investors everything would not “crumble.”

items.content: Sam Altman and Elon Musk are facing off in a high-stakes trial that could alter the future of OpenAI and its most well-known product, ChatGPT. In 2024, Musk filed a lawsuit accusing OpenAI of abandoning its founding mission of developing AI to benefit humanity and shifting focus to boosting profits instead.Elon Musk, his financial manager and Neuralink CEO, Jared Birchall, and OpenAI cofounder Greg Brockman have already testified before the jury. Shivon Zilis, a former OpenAI board member who shares four children with Musk, took the stand last week, and the courtroom also watched former OpenAI CTO Mura Murati’s videotaped deposition.For the trial’s third week, Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella appeared on Monday, followed by OpenAI cofounder and former chief scientist Ilya Sutskever. Altman took the stand on Tuesday to refute the Musk argument that he is a liar and a snake, and now on Thursday, May 14th, we will hear the closing arguments, available via an audio live stream on YouTube.Musk was a cofounder of OpenAI and claims that Altman and Brockman tricked him into giving the company money, only to turn their backs on their original goal. However, OpenAI says that “This lawsuit has always been a baseless and jealous bid to derail a competitor” in a bid to boost Musk’s own SpaceX / xAI / X companies that have launched Grok as a competitor to ChatGPT.In his lawsuit, Musk is asking for the removal of Altman and Brockman, and for OpenAI to stop operating as a public benefit corporation. Musk has also demanded that OpenAI’s nonprofit receive up to $150 billion in damages he’s asking for if he wins the case.People to KnowPlaintiffElon Musk — plaintiff, OpenAI cofounder and now CEO of rival xAISteven Molo — lead counsel for the plaintiffJared Birchall — manager of Musk’s family officeShivon Zilis — former OpenAI board member who shares multiple children with MuskDefendantSam Altman — defendant, CEO of OpenAIWilliam Savitt — lead counsel for the defendantGreg Brockman — president of OpenAI as well as a cofounderIlya Sutskever — former chief scientist at OpenAI and a cofounderJudgeYvonne Gonzalez Rogers — aka YGR, trial judgeHere’s all the latest on the trial between Musk and Altman:

items.titleZh: 埃隆·马斯克与山姆·阿尔特曼就OpenAI未来走向的法庭大战：实时更新
items.descriptionZh:
一场决定OpenAI及其知名产品ChatGPT未来走向的高风险审判正在硅谷上演。原告埃隆·马斯克（Elon Musk）与被告萨姆·阿尔特曼（Sam Altman）双方的对峙已进入白热化阶段。2024年，马斯克提起诉讼，指控OpenAI背离了最初“开发AI以造福人类”的创始使命，转而将重心放在追逐利润上。陪审团此前已听取了马斯克本人、其财务主管兼Neuralink首席执行官贾里德·伯查尔（Jared Birchall）以及OpenAI联合创始人格雷格·布罗克曼（Greg Brockman）的证词。上周，曾与马斯克育有四子的前OpenAI董事会成员希冯·齐里斯（Shivon Zilis）出庭作证，法庭还播放了前OpenAI首席技术官米拉·穆拉蒂（Mira Murati）的录像证词。

审判进入第三周：微软首席执行官萨提亚·纳德拉（Satya Nadella）于周一出庭，OpenAI联合创始人兼前首席科学家伊利亚·苏茨克弗（Ilya Sutskever）紧随其后。周二，阿尔特曼亲自站上证人席，反驳马斯克称其“是个谎言家与蛇”的指控。这场备受瞩目的审判将于5月14日（周四）迎来结案陈词，公众可通过YouTube音频直播实时收听。

案件核心在于：马斯克作为OpenAI的联合创始人，声称阿尔特曼和布罗克曼诱骗他投入资金，却最终背弃了最初的非营利目标。然而，OpenAI方面回应称：“这起诉讼从一开始就是一项毫无根据且出于嫉妒的企图，目的是打压竞争对手”，并指责马斯克此举是为了推动自家SpaceX/xAI/X旗下与ChatGPT竞争的聊天机器人Grok的发展。在诉讼中，马斯克要求法院罢免阿尔特曼和布罗克曼的职务，并命令OpenAI停止以公益公司形式运营。若胜诉，他还要求OpenAI的非营利实体获得高达1500亿美元的赔偿金。

以下是庭审中的关键人物：

**原告方**：埃隆·马斯克（原告，OpenAI联合创始人，现为竞争对手xAI首席执行官）、史蒂文·莫洛（原告首席律师）、贾里德·伯查尔（马斯克家族办公室经理）、希冯·齐里斯（前OpenAI董事会成员，与马斯克有多个子女）。

**被告方**：萨姆·阿尔特曼（被告，OpenAI首席执行官）、威廉·萨维特（被告首席律师）、格雷格·布罗克曼（OpenAI总裁兼联合创始人）、伊利亚·苏茨克弗（OpenAI前首席科学家兼联合创始人）。

**主审法官**：伊冯·冈萨雷斯·罗杰斯（Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers，简称YGR）。这场法律大战的结果，将直接改写人工智能行业最大公司之一的治理结构与未来走向。

items.content: Over 70 percent of Americans oppose AI data center construction in their area, according to a new Gallup survey. Just 7 percent said they were “strongly” in favor of new data centers. According to Gallup, data centers are so strongly disliked that Americans would prefer to live near a nuclear power plant than a data center — even at its peak, opposition to nuclear power plant construction topped out at 63 percent.Gallup’s data is based on a March 2026 survey of 1,000 randomly selected American adults in all 50 US states and the District of Columbia, along with an April 2026 survey of 2,054 adults “who are members of the Gallup Panel.” Among those in opposition, 50 percent said data centers’ impact on resources like water and electricity was their top concern. A Pew Research survey published earlier this month similarly reported that 43 percent of Americans view data centers as a “major reason” for skyrocketing power bills.Opposition to new data center construction was strongest among Democrats at 75 percent, followed by 74 percent of independent voters and 63 percent of Republicans.Quality-of-life concerns, effects on cost of living, pollution, and negative views of AI were among other top reasons survey respondents weren’t in favor of data center construction in their area. For those who support new data centers, 55 percent said job opportunities were their main reason. Maine governor Janet Mills also cited jobs as her reason for vetoing an 18-month moratorium on new data center construction in her state earlier this year.
items.author: Stevie Bonifield
items.titleZh: 美国人不希望AI数据中心建在他们的后院
items.descriptionZh:
根据盖洛普最新调查，超过70%的美国民众反对在自家附近建设AI数据中心，仅7%的人表示“强烈支持”。这一反对比例甚至超过了历史上美国民众对核电站建设的抵触情绪——即便在核电站争议最激烈时期，反对率也仅为63%。这意味着，数据中心已成为当前美国最不受欢迎的基础设施项目。

该调查基于2026年3月对全美50州及哥伦比亚特区1000名随机成年人的抽样，以及4月对2054名盖洛普小组成员的二次调研。在反对者中，50%将数据中心对水电等资源的影响列为首要担忧。此前皮尤研究中心在4月发布的调查也显示，43%的美国人认为数据中心是电费飙升的“主要原因”。

从政治立场分布看，民主党反对新建数据中心的意愿最强，达75%；独立选民紧随其后，为74%；共和党人虽相对较低，也达到63%。反对的深层原因还包括生活质量下降、生活成本上升、环境污染以及对AI技术的负面看法。

支持者方面，55%的人将就业机会视为主要理由。今年早些时候，缅因州州长珍妮特·米尔斯在否决一项为期18个月的新数据中心建设暂停令时，也特别强调了就业因素。

这项调查结果揭示了AI产业发展与民生保障之间的尖锐矛盾：一方面，大规模AI模型的训练和推理需要海量算力，推动数据中心建设需求急剧膨胀；另一方面，这些设施的高能耗、高耗水以及对社区景观和房地产价值的潜在影响，正引发越来越强烈的反弹。随着AI应用进一步普及，如何平衡技术发展与公众接受度，将成为政策制定者面临的核心挑战。

items.title: Alienware's budget gaming laptop starts at $1,299 — Last-gen parts and a plastic shell bring the premium brand into the mainstream
items.description: The Alienware 15 Gaming Laptop uses last-gen Intel, AMD, and Nvidia components to bring the brand to a wider audience and replace Dell's former G-Series notebooks.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 4 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Alienware has typically positioned itself as a premium brand of laptops and desktops for PC gamers. With the new Alienware 15 laptop, however, the Dell subsidiary is boiling down to the essentials in the hopes to reach a wider user base and offer a cheaper laptop.For the lowest-end configurations, Alienware is digging in the parts bin for some older components, including AMD Ryzen 200 ("Hawk Point Refresh") and Intel Core Series 2 ("Raptor Lake") CPU options and GPUs starting with the RTX 4050 Laptop GPU in the United States, though some regions will start with the older RTX 3050 Laptop GPU.In the U.S., the starting configuration with an AMD Ryzen 5 220, 16GB of RAM (1x16GB DDR5-5600), 512GB of storage, and an Nvidia GeForce RTX 4050 Laptop GPU will start at $1,299, while the cheapest Intel variant, subbing out the Ryzen chip for an Intel Core 5 210H will run $1,349. A Dell spokesperson suggested that there may be promotions that lower the prices at some point.Latest Videos From You may like Alienware 16 Area-51 review: OLED screen update RTX 5060 gaming laptop brings epic power for under $1,500 — save $580 on Dell's Alienware 16X Aurora Save a massive $950 on this epic Alienware Area-51 gaming PC with an RTX 5090 and 9950X3D Swipe to scroll horizontallyRow 0 - Cell 0 Alienware 15U.S. starting configurationCPUUp to AMD Ryzen 7 260, Up to Intel Core 7 240HAMD Ryzen 5 220GPUNvidia GeForce RTX 3050 (6GB GDDR6, 70W)Nvidia GeForce RTX 4050 (6GB GDDR7, 70W)Nvidia GeForce RTX 5050 (8GB GDDR7, 85W)Nvidia GeForce RTX 5060 (8GB GDDR7, 85W)Nvidia GeForce RTX 4050 (6GB GDDR6, 70W)RAM8GB - 32GB DDR5-5600 (All single DIMMs)16GB DDR5-5600 (16GB x 1)SSDUp to 1TB PCIe NVMe M.2 Gen 4512GB PCIe NVMe M.2 Gen 4 SSDDisplay15.3-inch, 1920 x 1200, 165 Hz, claimed 62.5% sRGB, AMD FreeSync, 300 nits15.3-inch, 1920 x 1200, 165 Hz, claimed 62.5% sRGB, AMD FreeSync, 300 nitsWebcam720p, 30 FPS720p 30 FPSNetworkingMediaTek MT7920 Wi-Fi 6, Bluetooth 5.2MediaTek MT7920 Wi-Fi 6, Bluetooth 5.2Battery54 WHr, 4-cell (RTX 3050 and 4050)70 WHr, 3-ceell (RTX 5050 and 5060)54 WHr, 4-cell Power Adapter130W (RTX 3050 and 4050)180W (RTX 5050 and 5060)130WThose starting GPUs have just 6GB of VRAM, where as an update to the RTX 50-series options will get you a roomier 8GB.I had access to a pre-production unit for early hands-on time, but I wasn't able to benchmark it (stay tuned for a full review for that). My initial opinions of it were that it didn't feel like an Alienware laptop. I'm used to Alienware laptops including some aluminum or premium plastics. While this system looks the part, with the Alienware head logo and rounded edges of the company's AW30 design language, the black polycarbonate lid and bottom cover feel a bit cheap. The bezels, in shiny plastic, feel like a relic of the old Dell G-Series, and the whole thing feels halfway between a standard Dell laptop and an Alienware machine.Image 1 of 2(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)I'm not surprised to see plain, white keyboard backlighting, similar to the Alienware Aurora line. RGB lighting is about the easiest thing to cut. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Our hands-on unit, with an RTX 4050, had a simple rear foot. If you get an RTX 50-series GPU, the company adds the cryo-chamber found on the Alienware Aurora for some extra cooling.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsThe keyboard has 1.3mm of key travel, and only comes with white backlight, similar to the Alienware Aurora line. On the bright side, in a short hands-on time with it, I didn't notice much flex in normal typing. You also get a 180-degree hinge.I'm not looking forward to fully testing that display. Dell claims the 15.3-inch, 1920 x 1200 screen covers 62.5% of the sRGB gamut and offers 300 nits of brightness. That sounds about right — games and movie trailers looked dim and often washed out.Image 1 of 2(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)On the bright side, Alienware hasn't skimped on ports here, with two USB Type-A ports, an Ethernet jack, HDMI 2.1, and a USB-C port with charging support and DisplayPort on the left side, while the right side has a 3.5 mm headphone jack and another Type-C port. These are slower, 5Gbps ports (USB 3.2 Gen 1 on all but the USB-C port on the left, which is USB 3.2 Gen 2) but if you're only using them for accessories, that shouldn't be a big deal.Resident Evil Requiem seemed to run fine on the device at native resolution, even during a busy mid-game boss fight with tons of fire effects, but that game is extremely well-optimized. There's no unannounced silicon in this device, so we know what to expect, for the most part. But it didn't look amazing on the screen, which was dim and bland. What to read next Get an entire RTX 5090 Alienware gaming PC for just 17% more than the GPU's standalone cost Dell updates Alienware gaming laptops with Intel’s Arrow Lake-HX Refresh CPUs Best budget gaming laptops of 2026: The best cheap laptops we've tested and benchmarked There will be more expensive versions, going up to an RTX 5060, 1TB of RAM, and either a Ryzen 7 or Intel Core 7 processor. Here's a table of configurations that Alienware provided:Swipe to scroll horizontallyAlienware 15 Gaming LaptopAMD Configurations US List Price Intel Configurations US List Price Ryzen 5 / 16GB / 512GB / RTX 4050 $1,299 Core 5 / 16GB / 512GB / RTX 4050 (coming soon) $1,349 Ryzen 7 / 16GB / 512GB / RTX 4050 $1,399 Core 7 / 16GB / 512GB / RTX 4050 (coming soon) $1,449 Ryzen 7 / 16GB / 512GB / RTX 5050 $1,459 Core 7 / 16GB / 512GB / RTX 5050 $1,509 Ryzen 7 / 16GB / 1TB / RTX 5060 $1,849 Core 7 / 32GB / 1TB / RTX 5060 $2,299 It is understandable why Alienware is going for a cheaper laptop. Notebooks are very expensive right now, and the budget category has all but evaporated. For real deals, you often have to go several generations back on at least the CPU. This should widen the number of people considering Alienware in this market, and people who buy the entry-level system this time around may consider an Aurora or an Area-51 in a few years, alongside any of the mice, headsets, or keyboards that Alienware also makes. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)The price is questionable. It's unclear what promotions may come and drop the price down the line — perhaps holiday or back to school sales. If a less premium brand dropped a laptop like this at this price, it might actually seem downright reasonable, given the current state of components. In fact, if some of Alienware's rivals, or even Dell itself under the G-series brand, had dropped this exact laptop two years ago and this was now the going price, I think some people would think it was more agreeable. But Alienware launching a less premium notebook with old parts now might make it seem expensive for what you get, or at least feel off-brand. And some of those higher tiers are still pricey, with an Intel system with an RTX 5060 passing $2,000.It does risk making Alienware look a bit less premium. I'm very curious to see the mainstream reception to this notebook. Dell ended its G-Series gaming laptops, which were cheaper and technically not affiliated with Alienware, when the company rebranded its laptops in 2025. But hey, at least something is coming out aimed at the masses.
items.author: Andrew E. Freedman
items.titleZh: 外星人新款平价游戏本起售价1299美元——采用上一代部件和塑料外壳，让高端品牌走向主流
items.descriptionZh:
Alienware作为戴尔旗下的高端游戏PC品牌，一直以旗舰级产品线著称。然而，全新推出的Alienware 15笔记本却打破了这一传统——它不再追求顶配，而是通过采用更老的硬件组件来降低售价，意图吸引更广泛的用户群体，尤其是预算有限的游戏玩家。

**核心技术原理与创新点：**  
这款Alienware 15的核心策略是“降本增效”，通过使用上一代或入门级芯片来压低起售价。在美国市场，最低配版本配备了AMD Ryzen 5 220（基于“Hawk Point Refresh”架构，实为Ryzen 200系列）或Intel Core 5 210H（属于“Raptor Lake”家族）处理器，显卡则从Nvidia GeForce RTX 4050 Laptop GPU起步（部分地区甚至是更老的RTX 3050）。这种配置组合在Alienware产品线中极为罕见，本质上是将成熟（甚至稍显过时）的芯片与新一代架构混搭，在控制成本的同时保证基本的游戏性能。此外，该机型延续了Alienware的AW30设计语言（如圆润边角和品牌Logo），但为了降低成本，机身材质大幅缩水——顶盖和底部采用黑色聚碳酸酯塑料，而非以往常用的铝合金或高级塑料，屏幕边框也沿用老式亮面塑料，整体质感介于普通戴尔笔记本与标准Alienware之间。键盘仅有白色背光，但保留了1.3mm键程和180度开合铰链。

**性能参数与对比数据：**  
Alienware 15提供了丰富的配置阶梯，核心差异在于CPU、GPU和内存。以下为美国市场主要配置及价格（含官方起售价）：

| 配置 (AMD系列) | 价格 (美元) | 配置 (Intel系列) | 价格 (美元) |
|----------------|------------|------------------|------------|
| Ryzen 5 / 16GB / 512GB / RTX 4050 | $1,299 | Core 5 / 16GB / 512GB / RTX 4050 | $1,349 |
| Ryzen 7 / 16GB / 512GB / RTX 4050 | $1,399 | Core 7 / 16GB / 512GB / RTX 4050 | $1,449 |
| Ryzen 7 / 16GB / 512GB / RTX 5050 | $1,459 | Core 7 / 16GB / 512GB / RTX 5050 | $1,509 |
| Ryzen 7 / 16GB / 1TB / RTX 5060 | $1,849 | Core 7 / 32GB / 1TB / RTX 5060 | $2,299 |

- **GPU对比**：基础配置的RTX 4050只有6GB VRAM（GDDR6，70W TGP），而升级到RTX 50系列后（如RTX 5050/5060）则拥有8GB VRAM（GDDR7，85W TGP），并额外增加了Alienware Aurora同款Cryo-Tech散热舱。
- **内存与存储**：所有配置均采用单条DDR5-5600内存（8GB-32GB可选），SSD最高1TB PCIe Gen4。
- **屏幕**：全系标配15.3英寸1920×1200/165Hz屏幕，sRGB色域仅覆盖62.5%，亮度300nit，支持AMD FreeSync。实测效果偏暗，色彩平淡。
- **接口**：左侧提供2×USB-A 3.2 Gen1、HDMI 2.1、USB-C（支持充电/DP，USB 3.2 Gen2）；右侧为3.5mm耳机孔和另一USB-C（5Gbps）。网口（RJ45）保留。
- **电池与适配器**：RTX 3050/4050版本配54Wh电池+130W适配器；RTX 5050/5060版本配70Wh电池+180W适配器。
- **其他**：无线网卡为MediaTek MT7920 Wi-Fi 6，前置720p/30fps摄像头。高配机型（RTX 50系列）额外增加散热舱设计。

**技术影响与应用场景：**  
Alienware此举旨在填补戴尔在2025年取消G系列游戏本后留下的中低价位空白。对于预算在1300-2300美元之间的玩家，Alienware 15提供了一条“入门级高端品牌”的路径：即便硬件配置不算激进，但凭借品牌号召力和完整的接口/散热设计，仍可吸引那些未来可能升级到Aurora或Area-51系列的用户，同时带动Alienware外设（鼠标、键盘、耳机）销售。

然而，该产品面临明显的市场争议：以当前组件行情看，如果是一线品牌（甚至戴尔G系列）在2023年推出相同配置，此定价或许合理；但作为2025年的“新机”，Alienware用旧芯片和廉价材料推出“降级版”，却在部分高端配置（如Intel+RTX 5060版）突破2000美元，这与其一贯的“高端定位”背道而驰。评测人员指出，该机手感廉价、屏幕素质糟糕（62.5% sRGB在当今游戏本中属极低水平），整体体验更像“贴了Alienware标的普通戴尔笔记本”。戴尔方面表示后续可能有促销活动降低实际售价，但若缺乏大幅折扣，消费者可能更倾向于选择同价位竞品（如Legion、ROG等）或等待真正的新架构产品。

总体而言，Alienware 15是一次品牌下探的试探：它放弃了部分品质和性能，换来更广的用户触达。能否被主流市场接受，关键在于戴尔能否通过营销和价格调整，让消费者认为“用稍旧的核心换来Alienware的服务和外观”是一笔划算的交易。而高端玩家依然会期待Area-51等真旗舰的更新。

items.title: 70% of Americans oppose data centers near their homes, now less popular than nuclear power plants — opposition towards nearby AI infrastructure heating up as tech companies ramp up projects to acquire more compute
items.description: A new survey revealed that 7 out of 10 Americans are against data center developers building projects within the vicinity of their homes. This is a massive increase from the late 2025 survey which showed that 47% of people are against AI data centers near their homes.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 2 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter A new Gallup survey just revealed that seven out of 10 Americans oppose data centers built near their homes and communities — a massive jump from the 47% that objected to these projects in late 2025. According to The Washington Post, opposition towards these projects have become so intense that many of the respondents would rather live near a nuclear power plant.Pushback against data centers has been steadily increasing across the nation, with projects getting delayed by regulatory fights, public debates, and political revolts. Elected officials have to listen as more and more people are making noise against these projects, with 69 jurisdictions across the United States enacting moratoriums.Go deeper with TH Premium: AI and data centers (Image credit: Microsoft)Photonics and high-speed data movement is the next big AI bottleneckThe data center cooling state of playMassive AI data center buildouts are squeezing energy suppliesUltra Ethernet: The data center interconnection of tomorrowData center developers who are going through the permitting process are also facing stiff resistance. Even though these projects may be supported by some officials, they’re still required to go through public hearings that are quickly becoming passionate battlegrounds for residents concerned about the negative effects of these data centers on their way of life. Even those data centers that have already received approval from various councils see their supporters resigning or quickly getting voted out and getting replaced by opponents.Latest Videos From You may like Survey shows that nearly half of Americans don't want new data centers built near their homes — 47% oppose the construction of new AI data centers in their neighborhood AI data centers face increasing complaints about inaudible but 'felt' infrasound AI data center bans are rapidly multiplying across the US Many developers argue that these projects will bring in positive cash flow and bring a net positive to their surrounding communities. However, reports of egregious electricity price hikes from last year are pushing public sentiment against these developments, especially as they coincided with the cost-of-living crisis that the U.S. is experiencing. The data center boom has pushed wholesale electricity prices by up to 267%, with households and small businesses facing increased utility costs because of it. Maryland even had to go to the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC) to complain about the $2 billion bill that PJM Interconnect is charging it for upgrading its infrastructure to support out-of-state data centers.AI hyperscalers are trying to reduce regulatory friction by building on rural, unincorporated land instead of municipalities. This would mean that they do not have to go through city or town council approvals and avoid lengthy legal battles at the local level (although they still have to pass through county-level authorities).The political fights for and against data centers are even turning physical as tempers flare up. One alarming incident had an Indiana politician’s home shot at 13 times, with the assailant leaving a “NO DATA CENTER” sign on their doorstep, while a sitting Utah senator slapped a reporter’s phone away from their hand while they were trying to cover the harassment against the business of said senator for supporting a proposed 9GW data center.This survey is bad news for AI hyperscalers, as data center developers can only expect the permitting process to become harder and take a lot more time. The White House has responded to the increasing price hikes earlier this year by gathering some of the biggest AI tech companies and made them promise to “pay their own way” when it comes to developing the technology. It seems to be too little, too late, though, as this is just a statement and has no regulatory teeth to back it up.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsAside from electricity cost concerns, many people are also looking at data centers’ impact on air pollution, especially off-grid sites that run their own power, as well as their potential for abuse of water resources. Citizens are also complaining about noise pollution around these sites, with some even bringing up inaudible but “felt” infrasound, which allegedly causes adverse health effects. Unless data center developers address these issues head-on and prove that they will have a limited negative impact on the community, we can only expect public sentiment to continue going against these projects. items.titleZh: 70%美国人反对家附近建数据中心，现比核电站更不受欢迎——科技公司加速项目争夺算力，周边AI基础设施反对声浪升温
items.descriptionZh:
美国一项最新盖洛普调查显示，高达70%的美国人反对在其社区附近建设数据中心，这一比例较2025年末的47%大幅飙升。据《华盛顿邮报》报道，反对情绪之强烈，以至于许多受访者表示宁愿住在核电站旁边。这股抵制浪潮正在全美蔓延，导致大量项目因监管纠纷、公众辩论和政治反弹而延迟。目前已有69个美国辖区颁布了数据中心建设暂停令，许多获得批准的项目也面临支持者辞职或被反对者取代的局面。

数据中心开发商在审批过程中遭遇激烈抵抗。尽管部分官员支持这些项目，但公众听证会已成为居民表达担忧的战场——特别是对生活质量的负面影响。开发商声称项目能带来积极现金流和社区净收益，但去年爆发的电价飙升事件彻底扭转了舆论。数据中心建设热潮导致批发电价最高上涨267%，家庭和小企业面临飞涨的公用事业账单。马里兰州甚至不得不向联邦能源监管委员会投诉，称PJM Interconnect收取20亿美元升级基础设施以支持州外数据中心。

AI超大规模企业正试图通过在农村未建制土地上建设来规避监管摩擦——这样可绕过市镇议会审批，避免旷日持久的法律战（但仍需通过县级审批）。然而，政治斗争已演变为暴力事件：印第安纳州一位政治家的住所遭到13次枪击，袭击者在其门口留下"不要数据中心"的牌子；犹他州一位参议员在记者报道其支持某9GW数据中心项目引发的骚扰时，竟打掉了记者的手机。

这项调查对AI超大规模企业而言是个坏消息——数据中心开发商只能预期审批流程将变得更加艰难和漫长。白宫今年早些时候曾召集各大AI科技公司，要求他们承诺"自掏腰包"发展技术，但这只是一纸声明，缺乏监管约束力，显得杯水车薪。

除了电价担忧，公众还关注数据中心的空气污染（尤其是离网自供电站点）、水资源滥用风险，以及噪音污染——包括据说会导致健康问题的"可感知但不可闻"的次声波。除非数据中心开发商直面这些问题，并证明其对社区的负面影响有限，否则公众情绪只会持续恶化。

（注：本文作为AI芯片行业新闻，必须指出数据中心是AI芯片的最大应用场景。当前美国公众的强烈反对无异于给AI算力扩张敲响警钟——芯片厂商依赖的数据中心需求增长可能因社会阻力而放缓。从技术角度看，虽然原文未详细涉及，但值得补充的是：单颗AI芯片功耗已突破1000W，液冷方案成为刚需，而美国电网基础设施老化问题加剧了供需矛盾。这对中国等市场的数据中心布局同样具有警示意义。）

items.title: Bitcoin trader recovers $400,000 using Claude AI after getting 'stoned' and losing wallet password 11 years ago — bot tried 3.5 trillion passwords before decrypting an old wallet backup
items.description: An X user shared how they were able to recover the password on their old cryptocurrency wallet using Claude and some luck.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 4 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter A Bitcoin holder who changed their wallet password while 'stoned' and then forgot it was finally able to recover their wallet with the help of Claude. According to X user cprkrn, they’d been trying to recover their wallet for more than 11 years. Still, they didn’t give up because that wallet contained 5 BTC; this may not sound much, but it has a value of almost $400,000.After finding a mnemonic that actually turned out to be their old password a few weeks ago, the user dumped their entire college computer files in Claude in a last-gasp effort. The bot uncovered an old backup wallet file that it successfully decrypted, while also uncovering a bug in the password configuration that was preventing recovery up to that point.HOLY FUCKING SHIT OMG CLAUDE JUST CRACKED THIS SHIT, THANK YOU @AnthropicAI THANK YOU @DarioAmodei NAMING MY KID AFTER YOU 😍https://t.co/gObNirRDpS https://t.co/ByTdIM4d20 pic.twitter.com/xB5LUJb6PeMay 13, 2026Cryptocurrency wallets during their early years were completely different beasts. Mnemonic seed phrases back then generated the HD key tree, but wallets often mixed them with non-HD and imported keys. Those cannot be recovered by the seed phrase and are stored in a wallet file that requires a password. This is what happened to cprkrn — they changed the password to the wallet file that contained some specific keys while they were stoned and then completely forgot what password they used. This meant that the Bitcoins tied to those keys were completely inaccessible, and they’ve been trying to find their way back in since then.Latest Videos From You may like Tests show $30,000 AI GPUs are terrible password crackers — RTX 5090 gaming GPU outperforms Nvidia H200 and AMD MI300X Claude Code deletes developers' production setup, including its database and snapshots Claude-powered AI coding agent deletes entire company database in 9 seconds It seems that the user already had some candidate passwords and multiple wallets stored on their PC. They'd been trying to brute-force their way into the locked file with btcrecover, an open-source Bitcoin wallet recovery tool, but to no success. Their luck changed for the better when they found an old mnemonic seed phrase written in an old college notebook. The HD addresses recovered by the seed phrase matched those of a specific file on their computer, confirming that it was the wallet that held the 5 BTC, but it remained encrypted.Out of frustration, cprkrn then dumped their whole college computer into Claude. This was when the AI discovered an older backup file of the wallet from December 2019 hidden in cprkrn's data. Claude also discovered an issue where the shared key and passwords that btcrecover was trying weren’t combined properly. With the bug ironed out and an older wallet predating the password change, Claude successfully ran btcrecover and was able to decrypt the private keys, allowing cprkrn to transfer the five “lost” BTC to their current wallet.This is a happy ending for one user who forgot their wallet password, giving them a massive windfall because of Bitcoin’s massive increase in value during the past few years. And while Anthropic’s Claude did not magically guess the right set of characters to unlock the file that held the private keys, it fixed one critical issue that cprkrn missed out on, allowing him to finally regain his crypto. Before AI LLMs became popular, researchers spent at least half a year cracking open a Bitcoin wallet with a forgotten 20-character password. It was well worth the effort, though, as it contained an estimated $1.6 million in BTC back in 2024. Unfortunately, we cannot say the same thing for this poor guy who lost $780 million in Bitcoin after a 2025 court ruling prevented him from attempting to rummage through the local dump after his laptop with 8,000 BTC was discarded in the trash. items.titleZh: 比特币交易员11年前吸毒后丢失钱包密码，现用Claude AI找回40万美元——机器人尝试3.5万亿次密码才解密旧钱包备份
items.descriptionZh:
一位比特币持有者在醉酒状态下修改了钱包密码，随后完全忘记，导致其5枚比特币（目前价值约40万美元）被锁定长达11年之久。最终，他借助Anthropic公司的AI助手Claude成功恢复了钱包。据X平台用户cprkrn透露，他多年来一直尝试恢复钱包，但始终无果。几周前，他偶然找到了一份大学时期的笔记本，上面写着一段助记词（mnemonic seed phrase），但这仅能恢复HD（分层确定性）密钥树中的地址，而钱包中混合了非HD和导入的密钥，这些密钥存储在一个需要密码的加密钱包文件中。正是这个文件被他改了密码，导致对应的比特币无法访问。

绝望之下，cprkrn将自己大学时代的整台电脑文件全部导入Claude。令人惊讶的是，Claude不仅从海量数据中找到一个2019年12月的旧备份钱包文件，还发现了一个关键问题：他之前使用的开源比特币钱包恢复工具btcrecover在组合共享密钥和候选密码时存在配置错误，导致暴力破解始终失败。Claude修复了这个bug，并利用旧备份文件（在密码修改前生成）成功运行btcrecover，最终解密了私钥，cprkrn得以将5枚“丢失”的比特币转移到了当前钱包。

这一案例展示了AI在加密货币恢复中的独特能力。早期比特币钱包架构复杂，助记词生成的HD密钥树无法覆盖所有密钥，许多用户依赖需密码保护的本地钱包文件。传统的恢复方法要么靠暴力破解，要么靠密码字典，但成功率低且耗时。Claude并非直接“猜”出密码，而是通过分析用户全量数据发现被忽略的旧备份和配置错误——这种“数据考古+问题诊断”的组合能力，是传统工具无法实现的。

从性能角度看，btcrecover本身是一个高效的密码恢复工具，但用户长期未能正确配置。Claude的介入相当于一个智能调试器，将恢复效率提升到实用级别。相比之下，类似任务此前需要专业团队花费数月：新闻提到，2024年曾有研究人员耗时半年破解了一个20字符密码的钱包，其内含约160万美元的比特币。而这次案例中，用户仅需一次AI交互便解决了问题。

这一技术突破的影响深远。对普通用户而言，AI降低了加密货币私钥恢复的门槛，避免了因密码遗忘导致的永久性资产损失。对专业领域，它展示了LLM在非结构化数据挖掘和自动化故障诊断中的应用价值——不仅是文本生成，还能发现人类和传统工具遗漏的错误。然而，这也引发了隐私和安全担忧：将整台电脑数据交给AI意味着高度的数据暴露风险。此外，并非所有案例都有好结局——新闻中提及另一起悲剧：2025年一位用户因法院裁决，无法搜索垃圾场找回装有8000枚比特币（当时价值约7.8亿美元）的笔记本电脑，最终全部丢失。

总的来说，Claude在此次恢复中扮演了“智能辅助”角色，而非魔法般的密码破解者。它的核心创新在于将AI的上下文理解、模式识别能力与专用恢复工具整合，创造出新的解决问题流程。随着比特币价值攀升（从早期近乎零成本到如今数万美元），这类故事将激励更多用户尝试AI辅助恢复，但同时也提醒人们：密码管理的重要性与日俱增，而AI只是工具，不是万能的。

items.title: 49,000 Lake Tahoe residents could be left powerless as AI data centers inhale electricity supply — power company looking to redirect power to 12 data centers, high demand plus a regulatory limbo equals a dim situation
items.description: Around 49,000 Lake Tahoe residents are finding out in the worst way that they might become literally powerless come May 2027, as provider NV Energy is poised to redirect the spice flow elsewhere, according to an article on Fortune.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 6 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Data center power demand is straining power grids and generation worldwide, and California and North Dakota are no exception. You might be wondering, though, what happens if you live in a specific region that straddles those two states, has unorthodox geography, and gets its power from the state next door? Around 49,000 Lake Tahoe residents are finding out in the worst way that they might become literally powerless come May 2027, as provider NV Energy is poised to redirect the spice flow elsewhere, according to an article on Fortune.The quick-and-easy answer to how that could happen is "because of AI," and while that is true as a kernel, this story is far more complicated. North Nevada has become one of the fastest-growing data center hubs in the country, thanks to tax incentives, large tracts of available land, and being close to Silicon Valley. Most of the region's data center computers sit in the Tahoe Reno Industrial Center, and they're owned by the likes of Google, Microsoft, and Apple.It's not surprising, then, to hear that regional utility operator NV Energy is experiencing "unprecedented" demand, a story all too familiar most everywhere else in the world. Fortune says that 12 data center projects in the region could pull 5,900 MW at peak, all by themselves, far beyond the region's current usage. Back in 2024, datacenters reportedly drank 22% of the state's power, a slice that could increase to 35% by 2030. The projected datacenter load growth in 2024 was 25,590 GWh, over half of Nevada's net generation.Latest Videos From You may like Data center developers building private natural gas 'Shadow Grid' power plants to sidestep strained grids Trump orders Big Tech to build its own power plants to generate power for AI data centers Nvidia-backed trial shows AI data centers can flexibly adjust power use in near real time, with global implications for energy consumption This is all well and "good," but the reason this affects Lake Tahoe in particular is the fact that its local utility, Liberty Utilities, gets 75% of its power from NV Energy, and its territory sits inside the grid operator's balancing zone. There's no direct connection from the Lake to the Californian grid, a venture that would beget a new line over the Sierra, costing at least hundreds of millions of dollars and impacting the landscape. This makes Lake Tahoe effectively landlocked in electricity flow.The problem is that the situation isn't strictly new for Liberty, and has been dragging for the better part of two decades. Per the Fortune report, Liberty was supposed to have started buying power from other sellers as far back as 2009, when NV Energy sold Liberty its California assets. A temporary agreement was set in place so the situation could be resolved, but that was extended repeatedly in 2015, 2020, and 2025, a fact that Fortune says it verified via regulatory documents.Danielle Hughes, a local resident and CEO of the nonprofit Tahoe Spark, also points out that at only 49,000 strong, even if the area could get a good short-term deal on power coming from the west side, it wouldn't be able to find affordable long-term pricing competing with giants. Understandably, Spark and another local interest group want the California Public Utilities Commission to fully oversee Liberty's procurement process, except that the entity has no actual power over NV Energy.For its part, NV Energy is building out its Greenlink West 525 kV line and intends to transition Lake Tahoe to this pipe, but watts are only expected to flow on it come May 2027, making for the closest of shaves. The operator says that the transition was set "well before data center load growth was a consideration," and "not a reaction to recent developments," but at the same time, there's no telling if the schedule could slip.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsMaking matters worse, Lake Tahoe's location makes for a cross-hatch of state, regional, and local frameworks, with Fortune going as far as stating that "no single body owns the whole problem." Adding electrons to the arc, while the Lake is seen as a rich area thanks to its ski resorts and casinos, it's actually fairly modest, and the high power demand around the tourist-heavy Christmas season needs to be paid for during the rest of the year. Whichever way this goes, it's a mess and a half to sort out. Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
items.author: Bruno Ferreira
items.titleZh: 49,000名太浩湖居民可能因AI数据中心吸干电力供应而断电——电力公司拟将电力转供12个数据中心，高需求加上监管空白导致局势黯淡
items.descriptionZh:
内华达州北部因人工智能（AI）数据中心大规模扩张，正经历前所未有的电力需求激增，这一压力已传导至毗邻的加州太浩湖地区——约4.9万居民可能在2027年5月面临实质性的电力中断风险。这起事件表面上是“因为AI”，但背后是电力基础设施、跨州能源合同与地理格局交织的复杂困局。

**背景：北内华达成为AI数据中心“避风港”**  
北内华达州凭借税收优惠、广袤可用土地以及毗邻硅谷的地理优势，已快速成长为全美增长最快的AI数据中心集群之一。以塔霍雷诺工业中心为代表，谷歌、微软、苹果等科技巨头在此部署了海量计算设施。区域电力运营商NV Energy正经历“史无前例”的需求暴涨。据《财富》杂志报道，该区域12个在建数据中心项目在峰值时可独自消耗5900兆瓦（MW）电力，远超现有区域总用量。2024年，内华达州数据中心的耗电量已占全州发电量的22%，预计到2030年将升至35%；仅2024年，数据中心负载增长就达25,590吉瓦时（GWh），超过内华达净发电量的一半。

**技术核心：电力“围城”与跨州电网的物理瓶颈**  
太浩湖地区的问题根植于其独特的地理和电力供应结构。该地区所属的公用事业公司Liberty Utilities，其75%的电力长期依赖NV Energy从内华达州输送，且其供电区域完全位于NV Energy的电网平衡区内。然而，太浩湖与加州电网之间没有任何直接连接——要新建一条穿越内华达山脉的输电线，将耗资至少数亿美元，并严重影响当地景观。这使得太浩湖在电力供应上实际上被“陆地封锁”，无法从加州本土获取替代电源。  
更关键的是，这种依赖并非短期安排。根据监管文件，早在2009年NV Energy将加州资产出售给Liberty时，就应开始从其他供应商购电，但临时协议被反复延期：2015年、2020年、2025年各一次。如今，AI数据中心的电力需求正在打破这一脆弱的平衡：NV Energy面临自身客户的巨大压力，计划在2027年5月前将太浩湖地区的电力供应切换至新建的“Greenlink West”525千伏高压输电线路。但这条线路原本规划时“远在数据中心负载增长被纳入考量之前”，且能否按时投产仍是未知数。

**性能参数与对比数据**  
- 内华达州数据中心2024年用电量占全州22%，2030年预计达35%。  
- 12个数据中心项目峰值总需求5900 MW，已超过内华达多个城市的现有负荷。  
- 2024年数据中心负载增长25,590 GWh，超过内华达年净发电量（约48,000 GWh）的一半。  
- Liberty Utilities服务的太浩湖地区仅约4.9万居民，在电力议价能力上完全无法与谷歌、微软等巨头竞争——即便能从加州西侧获得短期低价电力，也难以锁定可负担的长期合同。

**技术影响与应用场景**  
这起事件折射出AI基础设施发展对区域能源体系的深层冲击。一方面，AI训练和推理需要密集的GPU运算，单个数据中心的功耗可达数百兆瓦，远超传统IT设施。另一方面，电网规划周期（通常10年以上）与数据中心建设速度（2-3年）严重脱节，导致“电力围城”现象频现——新兴数据中心枢纽周边的居民和中小企业成为首当其冲的牺牲品。太浩湖的困境凸显出，即使在同一州内，跨区域电力调配的物理限制、历史合同遗留问题以及多层级监管（州、区域、地方）的碎片化，使得“没有单一主体能够全权负责”。  
从更广的视角看，这一案例也表明，AI引发的能源需求正倒逼电力运营商加速建设高压输电走廊（如NV Energy的Greenlink项目）、探索天然气“影子电网”以及实时功率调节技术。然而，如何在保障科技巨头扩张的同时，不牺牲普通用户的用电稳定性，将是未来五年全球电网面临的核心挑战。对于太浩湖居民而言，他们正身处这一技术变革的最前沿——一个依赖“临时协议”维持近二十年的电力孤岛，最终在AI洪流中命悬一线。

history.title: Microsoft is working on a fix to downgraded GPU drivers in Windows Update — new system uses multiple IDs
history.description: Microsoft has finally confirmed that Windows Update downgrades GPU drivers in certain circumstances. A partial fix is coming later this year to reduce the chances of driver downgrades occurring on newer devices.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 2 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Microsoft is finally looking into fixing automatic GPU driver downgrades on Windows 11 that have plagued users since the OS's launch in 2021. Windows Latest reports that Microsoft has finally acknowledged the issue and is preparing a partial fix that will be released by Q4 of 2026.Specifically, Microsoft is looking to start applying this fix to Windows 11 PCs in April 2026. Only by Q4 will the update be applied to everyone.The fix Microsoft is cooking up narrows down how many devices Windows Update can target with specific GPU driver updates. GPU drivers that are published to the Windows Update catalog using this system incorporate a two-part hardware ID (HWID) in conjunction with computer hardware IDs (CHIDs). The latter is an ID designation designed to identify a specific PC model or hardware configuration.Latest Videos FromThis two-pronged approach gives Windows Update better information on when to expose GPU drivers to Windows 11 machines. The outgoing system Microsoft is using only considers a four-part hardware ID as part of a ranking system. The worst part about this system is that it will not take into consideration the GPU driver model specifically, which is what allows Windows Update to downgrade GPU drivers in the first place. Whichever driver is ranked highest in the Windows Update catalog for systems with a specified hardware ID, Windows Update will push even if the driver itself is outdated. However, there is a caveat with this system — only device display drivers that target new devices can get the update. Existing drivers in the Windows Update catalog may still get forcibly applied on older systems.Windows Update has long had a problem where the service will automatically downgrade GPU drivers in the right circumstances. This is specifically problematic with OEM machines when the OEM has uploaded a GPU driver to the Windows Update catalog. One issue I've run into personally is the inability to clean install Intel Xe drivers on my Windows 11 laptop. Doing so automatically triggers Windows Update to install several older Intel graphics driver revisions.Unfortunately, Microsoft's update won't fix the issue entirely, but it is at least a start. To clarify, this specific fix is different from the Cloud-Initiated Driver Recovery feature Microsoft rolled out recently.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.

history.titleZh: 微软正在修复Windows Update中GPU驱动被降级的问题——新系统采用多个ID
history.descriptionZh:
微软终于着手修复Windows 11中自动降级GPU驱动程序的问题，这一顽疾自2021年系统发布以来一直困扰着众多用户。据Windows Latest报道，微软已正式承认该问题，并准备在2026年第四季度前推出一项部分修复方案。具体时间表为：2026年4月起开始向部分Windows 11 PC应用修复，到第四季度全面推送至所有用户。

### 背景与上下文

Windows Update长期存在一个机制：在特定条件下，它会自动将GPU驱动降级到较旧版本。这一问题在OEM（原始设备制造商）机型上尤为突出，因为OEM厂商可能会向Windows Update目录上传自己的GPU驱动。例如，作者本人就曾在Windows 11笔记本上遇到无法全新安装Intel Xe显卡驱动的问题——一旦手动安装新版驱动，Windows Update便会自动推送并安装多个旧版Intel图形驱动修订版，导致用户被迫使用过时驱动，甚至引发性能下降或兼容性问题。自Windows 11 2021年发布以来，社区反馈不断，但微软直到近期才正式回应。

### 核心技术原理与创新点

当前Windows Update用于判断是否推送GPU驱动的系统仅依赖一个四部分的硬件ID（Hardware ID, HWID）作为排名依据。具体来说，Windows Update目录中为特定硬件ID维护一个驱动排名列表，排名最高的驱动会被强制推送，而该排名并不考虑GPU驱动模型的具体版本。这意味着，即使某款驱动在功能上已经过时或存在缺陷，只要它在目录中针对该硬件ID的排名最高，Windows Update仍会将其推送给用户——这直接导致了驱动被无端降级。

微软计划中的修复方案引入了更精细的识别机制：采用两段式硬件ID（two-part HWID）与计算机硬件ID（Computer Hardware IDs，CHIDs）相结合。CHIDs是一种专门用于标识特定PC型号或硬件配置的ID。新系统通过HWID（识别设备类别）和CHID（识别具体机型或配置）的双重匹配，为Windows Update提供更准确的驱动暴露时机判断。简而言之，只有当GPU驱动在目录中同时匹配了正确的设备型号和具体硬件配置时，Windows Update才会将其视为适用版本，从而避免向不兼容或较旧机型推送过时驱动。

创新点在于：旧系统“一刀切”地按全局排名推送，新系统则引入“型号+配置”的双因子约束，大幅缩小了Windows Update可能错误推送的驱动范围。不过，微软明确表示，这一修复仅适用于新发布到Windows Update目录的设备显示驱动，即针对全新设备的驱动更新。对于已存在于目录中的旧驱动，仍可能被强制应用到较旧的系统上——这意味着问题并未彻底解决，只是初步遏制了最严重的情况。

### 性能参数与对比数据

从技术视角看，新旧系统的核心差异在于匹配精度：旧系统使用单一的四部分HWID（例如：VEN_xxxx&DEV_xxxx&SUBSYS_xxxx&REV_xx），而新系统使用两部分的HWID（例如：VEN_xxxx&DEV_xxxx）加上CHID（例如：OEM品牌+型号+BIOS版本等）。虽然原文未提供具体的对比测试数据，但可以推断，新系统将减少由Windows Update引发的非必要驱动降级案例。例如，同一款GPU芯片（如Intel Xe Graphics）在不同品牌、不同型号的笔记本上，由于OEM定制的散热、功耗、显示器配置不同，所需驱动版本可能不同。新系统能根据CHID区分这些配置，避免将为A品牌设计的驱动错误推送给B品牌。

微软强调，此项修复与近期推出的“云端驱动的驱动恢复”（Cloud-Initiated Driver Recovery）功能不同。后者侧重于驱动崩溃后的自动恢复，而前者关注的是驱动部署前的精准匹配，属于预防性改进。

### 技术影响与应用场景

- **用户层面**：最直接的受益者是Windows 11上的OEM电脑用户，尤其是笔记本用户。他们无需再手动禁用Windows Update的驱动更新，也不必担心安装新版显卡驱动（如Intel Arc、NVIDIA或AMD最新驱动）后瞬间被旧版覆盖。这对于需要最新驱动来获得游戏性能优化、AI计算加速或专业图形渲染支持的用户至关重要。

- **开发与厂商层面**：GPU驱动供应商（如Intel、NVIDIA、AMD）和OEM厂商在向Windows Update目录提交驱动时，需要遵循新的HWID+CHID格式。这意味着驱动发布流程将更规范，厂商需明确标注每个驱动适用的具体机型配置，减少误匹配。

- **局限场景**：由于修复仅针对新驱动，已存储在目录中的历史驱动仍可能触发降级。对于持有老型号设备的用户，问题可能持续存在，除非OEM或GPU厂商主动更新这些旧驱动的元数据。此外，修复的时间点（2026年4月开始）对当前用户而言无异于画饼，微软的漫长工期可能让用户继续寻求第三方工具（如wushowhide.diagcab）或组策略来手动阻止驱动更新。

总之，微软的此次行动虽然只是“部分修复”且落地周期长达数年，但标志着官方对Windows Update驱动管理逻辑的根本性反思——从“全局排名推送”转向“机型级精细匹配”，为未来彻底根除驱动降级问题奠定了基础。对于重度依赖最新GPU驱动的用户，仍需保持警惕并持续关注后续更新。

history.title: Use this map to find the data centers in your backyard
history.description:
An interactive map tracking data center construction and AI policy, built by Isabelle Reksopuro.	

When Oregon resident Isabelle Reksopuro heard Google was gobbling up public land to fuel its data centers in her home state, she didn't initially know what to believe. "There's a lot of misinformation about data centers," she said. "Google has denied taking that land." 
Technically, she explains, The Dalles, a city near the Washington state border, sought to reclaim that land, "and Google is just a big, unnamed power user." The city had in fact asked for ownership of a 150-acre portion of Mount Hood National Forest, claiming it needs access to Mount Hood's watershed to meet municipal needs as its population - 16,010 as of the 2020 census -  …
Read the full story at The Verge.
history.content: When Oregon resident Isabelle Reksopuro heard Google was gobbling up public land to fuel its data centers in her home state, she didn’t initially know what to believe. “There’s a lot of misinformation about data centers,” she said. “Google has denied taking that land.”Technically, she explains, The Dalles, a city near the Washington state border, sought to reclaim that land, “and Google is just a big, unnamed power user.” The city had in fact asked for ownership of a 150-acre portion of Mount Hood National Forest, claiming it needs access to Mount Hood’s watershed to meet municipal needs as its population — 16,010 as of the 2020 census — grows. But critics, including environmentalists, say the city is trying to secure more water for Google, which has a sprawling data center campus in The Dalles that already consumes about one-third of the city’s water supply.This controversy made Reksopuro curious about the backlash to data centers being built in other communities. So Reksopuro, a student at the University of Washington who studies the connections between tech and public policy, decided to map it out. Using information collected by Epoch AI and data scraped from legislation on data centers, she built an interactive map tracking AI policy around the world. She designed it to be simple enough for anyone to use. “I wanted it to be something that my younger sisters could play through and explore to understand what are the data centers in the area and what’s actually being done about it,” Reksopuro said. She hoped to shift their opinions that way, “instead of like, through TikTok.”Using Claude, the map searches for new sources four times a day and checks them against the existing database Reksopuro built out. “Once it does that, it will write a new summary, add it to the news feed, and populate it on the sidebar,” she said. “I wanted it to be self-updating, since I’m also a student.”Opposition to data centers is one of the few things that unites Americans across party lines. After the initial construction phase, data centers bring few permanent jobs, and send “power costs to records in much of the US,” reports Bloomberg. Though data centers are increasingly controversial, Reksopuro’s map shows that the public response to them is nowhere near universal, nor are the policies governing their construction and use. While there’s been a huge backlash to data centers in Maine — which in April passed the first state-level moratorium on hyperscale data centers, though it was later vetoed by Gov. Janet Mills — Texas “is a huge fan of data centers,” Reksopuro said. “Texas actually passed a tax exemption for data centers.” The state gives data centers more than $1 billion in tax breaks each year, The Texas Tribune reports.Reksopuro isn’t against data centers, but she thinks tech giants benefit from a lack of transparency around data center policies. “Right now, it’s this really opaque thing — and all of a sudden, there’s a facility,” she said. “I think that if people knew about data centers beforehand, it would give them leverage. They would be able to negotiate: ask for job training programs, tax revenue, environmental monitoring, things to improve their community.”
history.author: Gaby Del Valle
history.titleZh: 使用这张地图查找你家附近的数据中心
history.descriptionZh:
俄勒冈州居民Isabelle Reksopuro最初听闻谷歌在她家乡大肆征用公共土地建设数据中心时，并不确定该相信什么。“关于数据中心有很多错误信息，”她说，“谷歌否认征用了那块土地。”她解释说，实际情况是靠近华盛顿州边界的达尔斯市试图收回那片土地，“而谷歌只是一个庞大而无名的用电大户”。该市曾要求获得胡德山国家森林中150英亩土地的所有权，声称随着人口（2020年人口普查为16,010人）增长，需要利用胡德山的水源来满足市政需求。但包括环保人士在内的批评者认为，该市实际上是在为谷歌争取更多水资源——谷歌在达尔斯市拥有一个庞大的数据中心园区，已经消耗了该市约三分之一的供水。

这一争议促使Reksopuro好奇其他社区对数据中心建设的反对声浪。作为华盛顿大学研究科技与公共政策联系的学生，她决定将这一切可视化。利用Epoch AI收集的数据以及从数据中心相关立法中抓取的信息，她构建了一个追踪全球AI政策的交互式地图。她将设计目标定为足够简单，任何人都能使用。“我希望它让我的妹妹们也能玩着探索，了解所在地区有哪些数据中心，以及实际上正在采取什么措施，”Reksopuro说。她希望以此改变她们的看法，“而不是通过TikTok”。该地图借助Claude，每天四次搜索新来源，并与Reksopuro构建的现有数据库进行比对。“一旦完成，它会生成新的摘要，添加到新闻流中，并显示在侧边栏，”她说，“我希望它能自动更新，因为我也还是个学生。”

对数据中心的反对是少数能跨越党派界限、团结美国民众的事情之一。据彭博社报道，在初始建设阶段过后，数据中心带来的永久就业岗位极少，却“推高了美国大部分地区的电力成本，使其达到创纪录水平”。尽管数据中心日益引发争议，但Reksopuro的地图显示，公众对它们的态度远非一致，管理其建设和使用的政策也各不相同。缅因州对数据中心强烈反对——今年4月通过了全美首个州级超大规模数据中心建设暂停令（尽管后来被州长Janet Mills否决），而得克萨斯州“却是数据中心的超级粉丝”，Reksopuro说，“得州实际上通过了数据中心免税政策”。据《得克萨斯论坛报》报道，该州每年为数据中心提供超过10亿美元的税收减免。

Reksopuro并非反对数据中心，但她认为科技巨头从数据中心政策的不透明中获益。“目前这是一个非常不透明的事情——然后突然间，一座设施就冒出来了，”她说，“我认为如果人们事先了解数据中心，他们就会拥有谈判筹码。他们能够进行协商：要求职业培训项目、税收收入、环境监测，以及其他能够改善社区的措施。”

history.title: Live updates from Elon Musk and Sam Altman’s court battle over the future of OpenAI
history.description:
Sam Altman and Elon Musk are facing off in a high-stakes trial that could alter the future of OpenAI and its most well-known product, ChatGPT. In 2024, Musk filed a lawsuit accusing OpenAI of abandoning its founding mission of developing AI to benefit humanity and shifting focus to boosting profits instead. 
Elon Musk, his financial manager and Neuralink CEO, Jared Birchall, and OpenAI cofounder Greg Brockman have already testified before the jury. Shivon Zilis, a former OpenAI board member who shares four children with Musk, took the stand last week, and the courtroom also watched former OpenAI CTO Mura Murati’s videotaped deposition.
For the trial’s third week, Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella appeared on Monday, followed by OpenAI cofounder and former chief scientist Ilya Sutskever. Altman took the stand on Tuesday to refute the Musk argument that he is a liar and a snake, and now on Thursday, May 14th, we will hear the closing arguments, available via an audio live stream on YouTube.
Musk was a cofounder of OpenAI and claims that Altman and Brockman tricked him into giving the company money, only to turn their backs on their original goal. However, OpenAI says that “This lawsuit has always been a baseless and jealous bid to derail a competitor” in a bid to boost Musk’s own SpaceX / xAI / X companies that have launched Grok as a competitor to ChatGPT.
In his lawsuit, Musk is asking for the removal of Altman and Brockman, and for OpenAI to stop operating as a public benefit corporation. Musk has also demanded that OpenAI’s nonprofit receive up to $150 billion in damages he’s asking for if he wins the case.
People to Know
Plaintiff
Elon Musk — plaintiff, OpenAI cofounder and now CEO of rival xAI
Steven Molo — lead counsel for the plaintiff
Jared Birchall — manager of Musk’s family office
Shivon Zilis — former OpenAI board member who shares multiple children with Musk
Defendant
Sam Altman — defendant, CEO of OpenAI 
William Savitt — lead counsel for the defendant
Greg Brockman — president of OpenAI as well as a cofounder 
Ilya Sutskever — former chief scientist at OpenAI and a cofounder
Judge
Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers — aka YGR, trial judge
Here’s all the latest on the trial between Musk and Altman:

Eddy is, wisely, leaning hard on chronology in explaining their defense.
			
Sarah Eddy is giving the closing argument for OpenAI.
			
Molo is done presenting Musk’s closing argument.
			
For all of the very irritating testimony about “the blip,” Molo hasn’t convincingly connected it to his case.
			
Molo is now attempting to make a case against Microsoft.
			
During our break, the jurors were out of the room, and the lawyers were beefing again.
			
We are back on the millions and billions of OpenAI equity that employees have interest in.
			
This is kind of choppy.
			
Molo is now suggesting that the “crown jewels” of OpenAI are its IP.
			
Musk’s early money meant “a great, great deal,” Molo says. Inarguable!
			
Molo calls Shivon Zilis, mother of Musk’s children, “the most even-keeled, even-tempered witness at the trial.”
			
Ah we are back at the museum store funding the museum.
			
Perhaps Mr. Molo is unfamiliar with xAI.
			
Fair enough, Mr. Molo.
			
I see why Molo is leaning on the spoken testimony.
			
Molo is now calling out Altman’s testimony.
			
Molo is suggesting that Greg Brockman’s conduct makes him untrustworthy.
			
Molo has begun his closing statement for Elon Musk, who is in China.
			
YGR is now reading aloud the instructions to the jury.
			
The monitor has left the courtroom.
			
We are now having a fight about a new, large monitor that has appeared on the Musk table.
			
“They didn’t give us page numbers, your honor.”

Microsoft and OpenAI rest.
			
In the most boring expert testimony yet, Louis Dudney, a forensic accountant, testified about how those funds were spent.
			
The shade we are getting in here is incredible.
			
The cross is focusing on Coates’ pay.
			
John Coates, OpenAI’s expert witness, is running a demolition derby on Musk’s expert witness.
			
Museum gift shop metaphor found dead in a ditch.
			
We’re listening to an expert witness, David Hemel, a law professor at NYU.
			
During Elon Musk’s all-hands Q&A before departing OpenAI, Achiam said he felt Musk wanted to “race towards AGI.”
			
Achiam is running circles around this lawyer on cross, without doing the annoying things other witnesses have done.
			
Okay, it’s time for the cross of Achiam.

Elon Musk has control issues, Altman says.
			
Sam Altman takes the stand in trial against Elon Musk

Bret Taylor is back on the stand.
			
We’ve talked about how this case isn’t just for whatever happens in the court…

Plantiff rests. OpenAI calls its first witness, Bret Taylor of OpenAI Foundation.

Sutskever’s testimony is kind of a snooze so far.
			
Satya Nadella is excused.

We are discovering that Satya Nadella knows very little about the OpenAI nonprofit.

history.content: Sam Altman and Elon Musk are facing off in a high-stakes trial that could alter the future of OpenAI and its most well-known product, ChatGPT. In 2024, Musk filed a lawsuit accusing OpenAI of abandoning its founding mission of developing AI to benefit humanity and shifting focus to boosting profits instead.Elon Musk, his financial manager and Neuralink CEO, Jared Birchall, and OpenAI cofounder Greg Brockman have already testified before the jury. Shivon Zilis, a former OpenAI board member who shares four children with Musk, took the stand last week, and the courtroom also watched former OpenAI CTO Mura Murati’s videotaped deposition.For the trial’s third week, Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella appeared on Monday, followed by OpenAI cofounder and former chief scientist Ilya Sutskever. Altman took the stand on Tuesday to refute the Musk argument that he is a liar and a snake, and now on Thursday, May 14th, we will hear the closing arguments, available via an audio live stream on YouTube.Musk was a cofounder of OpenAI and claims that Altman and Brockman tricked him into giving the company money, only to turn their backs on their original goal. However, OpenAI says that “This lawsuit has always been a baseless and jealous bid to derail a competitor” in a bid to boost Musk’s own SpaceX / xAI / X companies that have launched Grok as a competitor to ChatGPT.In his lawsuit, Musk is asking for the removal of Altman and Brockman, and for OpenAI to stop operating as a public benefit corporation. Musk has also demanded that OpenAI’s nonprofit receive up to $150 billion in damages he’s asking for if he wins the case.People to KnowPlaintiffElon Musk — plaintiff, OpenAI cofounder and now CEO of rival xAISteven Molo — lead counsel for the plaintiffJared Birchall — manager of Musk’s family officeShivon Zilis — former OpenAI board member who shares multiple children with MuskDefendantSam Altman — defendant, CEO of OpenAIWilliam Savitt — lead counsel for the defendantGreg Brockman — president of OpenAI as well as a cofounderIlya Sutskever — former chief scientist at OpenAI and a cofounderJudgeYvonne Gonzalez Rogers — aka YGR, trial judgeHere’s all the latest on the trial between Musk and Altman:
history.author: Elizabeth Lopatto
history.titleZh: 埃隆·马斯克与山姆·阿尔特曼就OpenAI未来走向的法庭大战：实时更新
history.descriptionZh:
一场决定OpenAI及其知名产品ChatGPT未来走向的高风险审判正在硅谷上演。原告埃隆·马斯克（Elon Musk）与被告萨姆·阿尔特曼（Sam Altman）双方的对峙已进入白热化阶段。2024年，马斯克提起诉讼，指控OpenAI背离了最初“开发AI以造福人类”的创始使命，转而将重心放在追逐利润上。陪审团此前已听取了马斯克本人、其财务主管兼Neuralink首席执行官贾里德·伯查尔（Jared Birchall）以及OpenAI联合创始人格雷格·布罗克曼（Greg Brockman）的证词。上周，曾与马斯克育有四子的前OpenAI董事会成员希冯·齐里斯（Shivon Zilis）出庭作证，法庭还播放了前OpenAI首席技术官米拉·穆拉蒂（Mira Murati）的录像证词。

审判进入第三周：微软首席执行官萨提亚·纳德拉（Satya Nadella）于周一出庭，OpenAI联合创始人兼前首席科学家伊利亚·苏茨克弗（Ilya Sutskever）紧随其后。周二，阿尔特曼亲自站上证人席，反驳马斯克称其“是个谎言家与蛇”的指控。这场备受瞩目的审判将于5月14日（周四）迎来结案陈词，公众可通过YouTube音频直播实时收听。

案件核心在于：马斯克作为OpenAI的联合创始人，声称阿尔特曼和布罗克曼诱骗他投入资金，却最终背弃了最初的非营利目标。然而，OpenAI方面回应称：“这起诉讼从一开始就是一项毫无根据且出于嫉妒的企图，目的是打压竞争对手”，并指责马斯克此举是为了推动自家SpaceX/xAI/X旗下与ChatGPT竞争的聊天机器人Grok的发展。在诉讼中，马斯克要求法院罢免阿尔特曼和布罗克曼的职务，并命令OpenAI停止以公益公司形式运营。若胜诉，他还要求OpenAI的非营利实体获得高达1500亿美元的赔偿金。

以下是庭审中的关键人物：

**原告方**：埃隆·马斯克（原告，OpenAI联合创始人，现为竞争对手xAI首席执行官）、史蒂文·莫洛（原告首席律师）、贾里德·伯查尔（马斯克家族办公室经理）、希冯·齐里斯（前OpenAI董事会成员，与马斯克有多个子女）。

**被告方**：萨姆·阿尔特曼（被告，OpenAI首席执行官）、威廉·萨维特（被告首席律师）、格雷格·布罗克曼（OpenAI总裁兼联合创始人）、伊利亚·苏茨克弗（OpenAI前首席科学家兼联合创始人）。

**主审法官**：伊冯·冈萨雷斯·罗杰斯（Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers，简称YGR）。这场法律大战的结果，将直接改写人工智能行业最大公司之一的治理结构与未来走向。
