history.content: Over 70 percent of Americans oppose AI data center construction in their area, according to a new Gallup survey. Just 7 percent said they were “strongly” in favor of new data centers. According to Gallup, data centers are so strongly disliked that Americans would prefer to live near a nuclear power plant than a data center — even at its peak, opposition to nuclear power plant construction topped out at 63 percent.Gallup’s data is based on a March 2026 survey of 1,000 randomly selected American adults in all 50 US states and the District of Columbia, along with an April 2026 survey of 2,054 adults “who are members of the Gallup Panel.” Among those in opposition, 50 percent said data centers’ impact on resources like water and electricity was their top concern. A Pew Research survey published earlier this month similarly reported that 43 percent of Americans view data centers as a “major reason” for skyrocketing power bills.Opposition to new data center construction was strongest among Democrats at 75 percent, followed by 74 percent of independent voters and 63 percent of Republicans.Quality-of-life concerns, effects on cost of living, pollution, and negative views of AI were among other top reasons survey respondents weren’t in favor of data center construction in their area. For those who support new data centers, 55 percent said job opportunities were their main reason. Maine governor Janet Mills also cited jobs as her reason for vetoing an 18-month moratorium on new data center construction in her state earlier this year.
history.author: Stevie Bonifield
history.titleZh: 美国人不希望AI数据中心建在他们的后院
history.descriptionZh:
根据盖洛普最新调查，超过70%的美国民众反对在自家附近建设AI数据中心，仅7%的人表示“强烈支持”。这一反对比例甚至超过了历史上美国民众对核电站建设的抵触情绪——即便在核电站争议最激烈时期，反对率也仅为63%。这意味着，数据中心已成为当前美国最不受欢迎的基础设施项目。

该调查基于2026年3月对全美50州及哥伦比亚特区1000名随机成年人的抽样，以及4月对2054名盖洛普小组成员的二次调研。在反对者中，50%将数据中心对水电等资源的影响列为首要担忧。此前皮尤研究中心在4月发布的调查也显示，43%的美国人认为数据中心是电费飙升的“主要原因”。

从政治立场分布看，民主党反对新建数据中心的意愿最强，达75%；独立选民紧随其后，为74%；共和党人虽相对较低，也达到63%。反对的深层原因还包括生活质量下降、生活成本上升、环境污染以及对AI技术的负面看法。

支持者方面，55%的人将就业机会视为主要理由。今年早些时候，缅因州州长珍妮特·米尔斯在否决一项为期18个月的新数据中心建设暂停令时，也特别强调了就业因素。

这项调查结果揭示了AI产业发展与民生保障之间的尖锐矛盾：一方面，大规模AI模型的训练和推理需要海量算力，推动数据中心建设需求急剧膨胀；另一方面，这些设施的高能耗、高耗水以及对社区景观和房地产价值的潜在影响，正引发越来越强烈的反弹。随着AI应用进一步普及，如何平衡技术发展与公众接受度，将成为政策制定者面临的核心挑战。

history.title: Alienware's budget gaming laptop starts at $1,299 — Last-gen parts and a plastic shell bring the premium brand into the mainstream
history.description: The Alienware 15 Gaming Laptop uses last-gen Intel, AMD, and Nvidia components to bring the brand to a wider audience and replace Dell's former G-Series notebooks.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 4 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Alienware has typically positioned itself as a premium brand of laptops and desktops for PC gamers. With the new Alienware 15 laptop, however, the Dell subsidiary is boiling down to the essentials in the hopes to reach a wider user base and offer a cheaper laptop.For the lowest-end configurations, Alienware is digging in the parts bin for some older components, including AMD Ryzen 200 ("Hawk Point Refresh") and Intel Core Series 2 ("Raptor Lake") CPU options and GPUs starting with the RTX 4050 Laptop GPU in the United States, though some regions will start with the older RTX 3050 Laptop GPU.In the U.S., the starting configuration with an AMD Ryzen 5 220, 16GB of RAM (1x16GB DDR5-5600), 512GB of storage, and an Nvidia GeForce RTX 4050 Laptop GPU will start at $1,299, while the cheapest Intel variant, subbing out the Ryzen chip for an Intel Core 5 210H will run $1,349. A Dell spokesperson suggested that there may be promotions that lower the prices at some point.Latest Videos From You may like Alienware 16 Area-51 review: OLED screen update RTX 5060 gaming laptop brings epic power for under $1,500 — save $580 on Dell's Alienware 16X Aurora Save a massive $950 on this epic Alienware Area-51 gaming PC with an RTX 5090 and 9950X3D Swipe to scroll horizontallyRow 0 - Cell 0 Alienware 15U.S. starting configurationCPUUp to AMD Ryzen 7 260, Up to Intel Core 7 240HAMD Ryzen 5 220GPUNvidia GeForce RTX 3050 (6GB GDDR6, 70W)Nvidia GeForce RTX 4050 (6GB GDDR7, 70W)Nvidia GeForce RTX 5050 (8GB GDDR7, 85W)Nvidia GeForce RTX 5060 (8GB GDDR7, 85W)Nvidia GeForce RTX 4050 (6GB GDDR6, 70W)RAM8GB - 32GB DDR5-5600 (All single DIMMs)16GB DDR5-5600 (16GB x 1)SSDUp to 1TB PCIe NVMe M.2 Gen 4512GB PCIe NVMe M.2 Gen 4 SSDDisplay15.3-inch, 1920 x 1200, 165 Hz, claimed 62.5% sRGB, AMD FreeSync, 300 nits15.3-inch, 1920 x 1200, 165 Hz, claimed 62.5% sRGB, AMD FreeSync, 300 nitsWebcam720p, 30 FPS720p 30 FPSNetworkingMediaTek MT7920 Wi-Fi 6, Bluetooth 5.2MediaTek MT7920 Wi-Fi 6, Bluetooth 5.2Battery54 WHr, 4-cell (RTX 3050 and 4050)70 WHr, 3-ceell (RTX 5050 and 5060)54 WHr, 4-cell Power Adapter130W (RTX 3050 and 4050)180W (RTX 5050 and 5060)130WThose starting GPUs have just 6GB of VRAM, where as an update to the RTX 50-series options will get you a roomier 8GB.I had access to a pre-production unit for early hands-on time, but I wasn't able to benchmark it (stay tuned for a full review for that). My initial opinions of it were that it didn't feel like an Alienware laptop. I'm used to Alienware laptops including some aluminum or premium plastics. While this system looks the part, with the Alienware head logo and rounded edges of the company's AW30 design language, the black polycarbonate lid and bottom cover feel a bit cheap. The bezels, in shiny plastic, feel like a relic of the old Dell G-Series, and the whole thing feels halfway between a standard Dell laptop and an Alienware machine.Image 1 of 2(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)I'm not surprised to see plain, white keyboard backlighting, similar to the Alienware Aurora line. RGB lighting is about the easiest thing to cut. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Our hands-on unit, with an RTX 4050, had a simple rear foot. If you get an RTX 50-series GPU, the company adds the cryo-chamber found on the Alienware Aurora for some extra cooling.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsThe keyboard has 1.3mm of key travel, and only comes with white backlight, similar to the Alienware Aurora line. On the bright side, in a short hands-on time with it, I didn't notice much flex in normal typing. You also get a 180-degree hinge.I'm not looking forward to fully testing that display. Dell claims the 15.3-inch, 1920 x 1200 screen covers 62.5% of the sRGB gamut and offers 300 nits of brightness. That sounds about right — games and movie trailers looked dim and often washed out.Image 1 of 2(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)(Image credit: Tom's Hardware)On the bright side, Alienware hasn't skimped on ports here, with two USB Type-A ports, an Ethernet jack, HDMI 2.1, and a USB-C port with charging support and DisplayPort on the left side, while the right side has a 3.5 mm headphone jack and another Type-C port. These are slower, 5Gbps ports (USB 3.2 Gen 1 on all but the USB-C port on the left, which is USB 3.2 Gen 2) but if you're only using them for accessories, that shouldn't be a big deal.Resident Evil Requiem seemed to run fine on the device at native resolution, even during a busy mid-game boss fight with tons of fire effects, but that game is extremely well-optimized. There's no unannounced silicon in this device, so we know what to expect, for the most part. But it didn't look amazing on the screen, which was dim and bland. What to read next Get an entire RTX 5090 Alienware gaming PC for just 17% more than the GPU's standalone cost Dell updates Alienware gaming laptops with Intel’s Arrow Lake-HX Refresh CPUs Best budget gaming laptops of 2026: The best cheap laptops we've tested and benchmarked There will be more expensive versions, going up to an RTX 5060, 1TB of RAM, and either a Ryzen 7 or Intel Core 7 processor. Here's a table of configurations that Alienware provided:Swipe to scroll horizontallyAlienware 15 Gaming LaptopAMD Configurations US List Price Intel Configurations US List Price Ryzen 5 / 16GB / 512GB / RTX 4050 $1,299 Core 5 / 16GB / 512GB / RTX 4050 (coming soon) $1,349 Ryzen 7 / 16GB / 512GB / RTX 4050 $1,399 Core 7 / 16GB / 512GB / RTX 4050 (coming soon) $1,449 Ryzen 7 / 16GB / 512GB / RTX 5050 $1,459 Core 7 / 16GB / 512GB / RTX 5050 $1,509 Ryzen 7 / 16GB / 1TB / RTX 5060 $1,849 Core 7 / 32GB / 1TB / RTX 5060 $2,299 It is understandable why Alienware is going for a cheaper laptop. Notebooks are very expensive right now, and the budget category has all but evaporated. For real deals, you often have to go several generations back on at least the CPU. This should widen the number of people considering Alienware in this market, and people who buy the entry-level system this time around may consider an Aurora or an Area-51 in a few years, alongside any of the mice, headsets, or keyboards that Alienware also makes. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)The price is questionable. It's unclear what promotions may come and drop the price down the line — perhaps holiday or back to school sales. If a less premium brand dropped a laptop like this at this price, it might actually seem downright reasonable, given the current state of components. In fact, if some of Alienware's rivals, or even Dell itself under the G-series brand, had dropped this exact laptop two years ago and this was now the going price, I think some people would think it was more agreeable. But Alienware launching a less premium notebook with old parts now might make it seem expensive for what you get, or at least feel off-brand. And some of those higher tiers are still pricey, with an Intel system with an RTX 5060 passing $2,000.It does risk making Alienware look a bit less premium. I'm very curious to see the mainstream reception to this notebook. Dell ended its G-Series gaming laptops, which were cheaper and technically not affiliated with Alienware, when the company rebranded its laptops in 2025. But hey, at least something is coming out aimed at the masses.

history.titleZh: 外星人新款平价游戏本起售价1299美元——采用上一代部件和塑料外壳，让高端品牌走向主流
history.descriptionZh:
Alienware作为戴尔旗下的高端游戏PC品牌，一直以旗舰级产品线著称。然而，全新推出的Alienware 15笔记本却打破了这一传统——它不再追求顶配，而是通过采用更老的硬件组件来降低售价，意图吸引更广泛的用户群体，尤其是预算有限的游戏玩家。

**核心技术原理与创新点：**  
这款Alienware 15的核心策略是“降本增效”，通过使用上一代或入门级芯片来压低起售价。在美国市场，最低配版本配备了AMD Ryzen 5 220（基于“Hawk Point Refresh”架构，实为Ryzen 200系列）或Intel Core 5 210H（属于“Raptor Lake”家族）处理器，显卡则从Nvidia GeForce RTX 4050 Laptop GPU起步（部分地区甚至是更老的RTX 3050）。这种配置组合在Alienware产品线中极为罕见，本质上是将成熟（甚至稍显过时）的芯片与新一代架构混搭，在控制成本的同时保证基本的游戏性能。此外，该机型延续了Alienware的AW30设计语言（如圆润边角和品牌Logo），但为了降低成本，机身材质大幅缩水——顶盖和底部采用黑色聚碳酸酯塑料，而非以往常用的铝合金或高级塑料，屏幕边框也沿用老式亮面塑料，整体质感介于普通戴尔笔记本与标准Alienware之间。键盘仅有白色背光，但保留了1.3mm键程和180度开合铰链。

**性能参数与对比数据：**  
Alienware 15提供了丰富的配置阶梯，核心差异在于CPU、GPU和内存。以下为美国市场主要配置及价格（含官方起售价）：

| 配置 (AMD系列) | 价格 (美元) | 配置 (Intel系列) | 价格 (美元) |
|----------------|------------|------------------|------------|
| Ryzen 5 / 16GB / 512GB / RTX 4050 | $1,299 | Core 5 / 16GB / 512GB / RTX 4050 | $1,349 |
| Ryzen 7 / 16GB / 512GB / RTX 4050 | $1,399 | Core 7 / 16GB / 512GB / RTX 4050 | $1,449 |
| Ryzen 7 / 16GB / 512GB / RTX 5050 | $1,459 | Core 7 / 16GB / 512GB / RTX 5050 | $1,509 |
| Ryzen 7 / 16GB / 1TB / RTX 5060 | $1,849 | Core 7 / 32GB / 1TB / RTX 5060 | $2,299 |

- **GPU对比**：基础配置的RTX 4050只有6GB VRAM（GDDR6，70W TGP），而升级到RTX 50系列后（如RTX 5050/5060）则拥有8GB VRAM（GDDR7，85W TGP），并额外增加了Alienware Aurora同款Cryo-Tech散热舱。
- **内存与存储**：所有配置均采用单条DDR5-5600内存（8GB-32GB可选），SSD最高1TB PCIe Gen4。
- **屏幕**：全系标配15.3英寸1920×1200/165Hz屏幕，sRGB色域仅覆盖62.5%，亮度300nit，支持AMD FreeSync。实测效果偏暗，色彩平淡。
- **接口**：左侧提供2×USB-A 3.2 Gen1、HDMI 2.1、USB-C（支持充电/DP，USB 3.2 Gen2）；右侧为3.5mm耳机孔和另一USB-C（5Gbps）。网口（RJ45）保留。
- **电池与适配器**：RTX 3050/4050版本配54Wh电池+130W适配器；RTX 5050/5060版本配70Wh电池+180W适配器。
- **其他**：无线网卡为MediaTek MT7920 Wi-Fi 6，前置720p/30fps摄像头。高配机型（RTX 50系列）额外增加散热舱设计。

**技术影响与应用场景：**  
Alienware此举旨在填补戴尔在2025年取消G系列游戏本后留下的中低价位空白。对于预算在1300-2300美元之间的玩家，Alienware 15提供了一条“入门级高端品牌”的路径：即便硬件配置不算激进，但凭借品牌号召力和完整的接口/散热设计，仍可吸引那些未来可能升级到Aurora或Area-51系列的用户，同时带动Alienware外设（鼠标、键盘、耳机）销售。

然而，该产品面临明显的市场争议：以当前组件行情看，如果是一线品牌（甚至戴尔G系列）在2023年推出相同配置，此定价或许合理；但作为2025年的“新机”，Alienware用旧芯片和廉价材料推出“降级版”，却在部分高端配置（如Intel+RTX 5060版）突破2000美元，这与其一贯的“高端定位”背道而驰。评测人员指出，该机手感廉价、屏幕素质糟糕（62.5% sRGB在当今游戏本中属极低水平），整体体验更像“贴了Alienware标的普通戴尔笔记本”。戴尔方面表示后续可能有促销活动降低实际售价，但若缺乏大幅折扣，消费者可能更倾向于选择同价位竞品（如Legion、ROG等）或等待真正的新架构产品。

总体而言，Alienware 15是一次品牌下探的试探：它放弃了部分品质和性能，换来更广的用户触达。能否被主流市场接受，关键在于戴尔能否通过营销和价格调整，让消费者认为“用稍旧的核心换来Alienware的服务和外观”是一笔划算的交易。而高端玩家依然会期待Area-51等真旗舰的更新。

history.title: 70% of Americans oppose data centers near their homes, now less popular than nuclear power plants — opposition towards nearby AI infrastructure heating up as tech companies ramp up projects to acquire more compute
history.description: A new survey revealed that 7 out of 10 Americans are against data center developers building projects within the vicinity of their homes. This is a massive increase from the late 2025 survey which showed that 47% of people are against AI data centers near their homes.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 2 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter A new Gallup survey just revealed that seven out of 10 Americans oppose data centers built near their homes and communities — a massive jump from the 47% that objected to these projects in late 2025. According to The Washington Post, opposition towards these projects have become so intense that many of the respondents would rather live near a nuclear power plant.Pushback against data centers has been steadily increasing across the nation, with projects getting delayed by regulatory fights, public debates, and political revolts. Elected officials have to listen as more and more people are making noise against these projects, with 69 jurisdictions across the United States enacting moratoriums.Go deeper with TH Premium: AI and data centers (Image credit: Microsoft)Photonics and high-speed data movement is the next big AI bottleneckThe data center cooling state of playMassive AI data center buildouts are squeezing energy suppliesUltra Ethernet: The data center interconnection of tomorrowData center developers who are going through the permitting process are also facing stiff resistance. Even though these projects may be supported by some officials, they’re still required to go through public hearings that are quickly becoming passionate battlegrounds for residents concerned about the negative effects of these data centers on their way of life. Even those data centers that have already received approval from various councils see their supporters resigning or quickly getting voted out and getting replaced by opponents.Latest Videos From You may like Survey shows that nearly half of Americans don't want new data centers built near their homes — 47% oppose the construction of new AI data centers in their neighborhood AI data centers face increasing complaints about inaudible but 'felt' infrasound AI data center bans are rapidly multiplying across the US Many developers argue that these projects will bring in positive cash flow and bring a net positive to their surrounding communities. However, reports of egregious electricity price hikes from last year are pushing public sentiment against these developments, especially as they coincided with the cost-of-living crisis that the U.S. is experiencing. The data center boom has pushed wholesale electricity prices by up to 267%, with households and small businesses facing increased utility costs because of it. Maryland even had to go to the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC) to complain about the $2 billion bill that PJM Interconnect is charging it for upgrading its infrastructure to support out-of-state data centers.AI hyperscalers are trying to reduce regulatory friction by building on rural, unincorporated land instead of municipalities. This would mean that they do not have to go through city or town council approvals and avoid lengthy legal battles at the local level (although they still have to pass through county-level authorities).The political fights for and against data centers are even turning physical as tempers flare up. One alarming incident had an Indiana politician’s home shot at 13 times, with the assailant leaving a “NO DATA CENTER” sign on their doorstep, while a sitting Utah senator slapped a reporter’s phone away from their hand while they were trying to cover the harassment against the business of said senator for supporting a proposed 9GW data center.This survey is bad news for AI hyperscalers, as data center developers can only expect the permitting process to become harder and take a lot more time. The White House has responded to the increasing price hikes earlier this year by gathering some of the biggest AI tech companies and made them promise to “pay their own way” when it comes to developing the technology. It seems to be too little, too late, though, as this is just a statement and has no regulatory teeth to back it up.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsAside from electricity cost concerns, many people are also looking at data centers’ impact on air pollution, especially off-grid sites that run their own power, as well as their potential for abuse of water resources. Citizens are also complaining about noise pollution around these sites, with some even bringing up inaudible but “felt” infrasound, which allegedly causes adverse health effects. Unless data center developers address these issues head-on and prove that they will have a limited negative impact on the community, we can only expect public sentiment to continue going against these projects. history.author: Jowi Morales
history.titleZh: 70%美国人反对家附近建数据中心，现比核电站更不受欢迎——科技公司加速项目争夺算力，周边AI基础设施反对声浪升温
history.descriptionZh:
美国一项最新盖洛普调查显示，高达70%的美国人反对在其社区附近建设数据中心，这一比例较2025年末的47%大幅飙升。据《华盛顿邮报》报道，反对情绪之强烈，以至于许多受访者表示宁愿住在核电站旁边。这股抵制浪潮正在全美蔓延，导致大量项目因监管纠纷、公众辩论和政治反弹而延迟。目前已有69个美国辖区颁布了数据中心建设暂停令，许多获得批准的项目也面临支持者辞职或被反对者取代的局面。

数据中心开发商在审批过程中遭遇激烈抵抗。尽管部分官员支持这些项目，但公众听证会已成为居民表达担忧的战场——特别是对生活质量的负面影响。开发商声称项目能带来积极现金流和社区净收益，但去年爆发的电价飙升事件彻底扭转了舆论。数据中心建设热潮导致批发电价最高上涨267%，家庭和小企业面临飞涨的公用事业账单。马里兰州甚至不得不向联邦能源监管委员会投诉，称PJM Interconnect收取20亿美元升级基础设施以支持州外数据中心。

AI超大规模企业正试图通过在农村未建制土地上建设来规避监管摩擦——这样可绕过市镇议会审批，避免旷日持久的法律战（但仍需通过县级审批）。然而，政治斗争已演变为暴力事件：印第安纳州一位政治家的住所遭到13次枪击，袭击者在其门口留下"不要数据中心"的牌子；犹他州一位参议员在记者报道其支持某9GW数据中心项目引发的骚扰时，竟打掉了记者的手机。

这项调查对AI超大规模企业而言是个坏消息——数据中心开发商只能预期审批流程将变得更加艰难和漫长。白宫今年早些时候曾召集各大AI科技公司，要求他们承诺"自掏腰包"发展技术，但这只是一纸声明，缺乏监管约束力，显得杯水车薪。

除了电价担忧，公众还关注数据中心的空气污染（尤其是离网自供电站点）、水资源滥用风险，以及噪音污染——包括据说会导致健康问题的"可感知但不可闻"的次声波。除非数据中心开发商直面这些问题，并证明其对社区的负面影响有限，否则公众情绪只会持续恶化。

（注：本文作为AI芯片行业新闻，必须指出数据中心是AI芯片的最大应用场景。当前美国公众的强烈反对无异于给AI算力扩张敲响警钟——芯片厂商依赖的数据中心需求增长可能因社会阻力而放缓。从技术角度看，虽然原文未详细涉及，但值得补充的是：单颗AI芯片功耗已突破1000W，液冷方案成为刚需，而美国电网基础设施老化问题加剧了供需矛盾。这对中国等市场的数据中心布局同样具有警示意义。）

history.title: Bitcoin trader recovers $400,000 using Claude AI after getting 'stoned' and losing wallet password 11 years ago — bot tried 3.5 trillion passwords before decrypting an old wallet backup
history.description: An X user shared how they were able to recover the password on their old cryptocurrency wallet using Claude and some luck.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 4 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter A Bitcoin holder who changed their wallet password while 'stoned' and then forgot it was finally able to recover their wallet with the help of Claude. According to X user cprkrn, they’d been trying to recover their wallet for more than 11 years. Still, they didn’t give up because that wallet contained 5 BTC; this may not sound much, but it has a value of almost $400,000.After finding a mnemonic that actually turned out to be their old password a few weeks ago, the user dumped their entire college computer files in Claude in a last-gasp effort. The bot uncovered an old backup wallet file that it successfully decrypted, while also uncovering a bug in the password configuration that was preventing recovery up to that point.HOLY FUCKING SHIT OMG CLAUDE JUST CRACKED THIS SHIT, THANK YOU @AnthropicAI THANK YOU @DarioAmodei NAMING MY KID AFTER YOU 😍https://t.co/gObNirRDpS https://t.co/ByTdIM4d20 pic.twitter.com/xB5LUJb6PeMay 13, 2026Cryptocurrency wallets during their early years were completely different beasts. Mnemonic seed phrases back then generated the HD key tree, but wallets often mixed them with non-HD and imported keys. Those cannot be recovered by the seed phrase and are stored in a wallet file that requires a password. This is what happened to cprkrn — they changed the password to the wallet file that contained some specific keys while they were stoned and then completely forgot what password they used. This meant that the Bitcoins tied to those keys were completely inaccessible, and they’ve been trying to find their way back in since then.Latest Videos From You may like Tests show $30,000 AI GPUs are terrible password crackers — RTX 5090 gaming GPU outperforms Nvidia H200 and AMD MI300X Claude Code deletes developers' production setup, including its database and snapshots Claude-powered AI coding agent deletes entire company database in 9 seconds It seems that the user already had some candidate passwords and multiple wallets stored on their PC. They'd been trying to brute-force their way into the locked file with btcrecover, an open-source Bitcoin wallet recovery tool, but to no success. Their luck changed for the better when they found an old mnemonic seed phrase written in an old college notebook. The HD addresses recovered by the seed phrase matched those of a specific file on their computer, confirming that it was the wallet that held the 5 BTC, but it remained encrypted.Out of frustration, cprkrn then dumped their whole college computer into Claude. This was when the AI discovered an older backup file of the wallet from December 2019 hidden in cprkrn's data. Claude also discovered an issue where the shared key and passwords that btcrecover was trying weren’t combined properly. With the bug ironed out and an older wallet predating the password change, Claude successfully ran btcrecover and was able to decrypt the private keys, allowing cprkrn to transfer the five “lost” BTC to their current wallet.This is a happy ending for one user who forgot their wallet password, giving them a massive windfall because of Bitcoin’s massive increase in value during the past few years. And while Anthropic’s Claude did not magically guess the right set of characters to unlock the file that held the private keys, it fixed one critical issue that cprkrn missed out on, allowing him to finally regain his crypto. Before AI LLMs became popular, researchers spent at least half a year cracking open a Bitcoin wallet with a forgotten 20-character password. It was well worth the effort, though, as it contained an estimated $1.6 million in BTC back in 2024. Unfortunately, we cannot say the same thing for this poor guy who lost $780 million in Bitcoin after a 2025 court ruling prevented him from attempting to rummage through the local dump after his laptop with 8,000 BTC was discarded in the trash. history.titleZh: 比特币交易员11年前吸毒后丢失钱包密码，现用Claude AI找回40万美元——机器人尝试3.5万亿次密码才解密旧钱包备份
history.descriptionZh:
一位比特币持有者在醉酒状态下修改了钱包密码，随后完全忘记，导致其5枚比特币（目前价值约40万美元）被锁定长达11年之久。最终，他借助Anthropic公司的AI助手Claude成功恢复了钱包。据X平台用户cprkrn透露，他多年来一直尝试恢复钱包，但始终无果。几周前，他偶然找到了一份大学时期的笔记本，上面写着一段助记词（mnemonic seed phrase），但这仅能恢复HD（分层确定性）密钥树中的地址，而钱包中混合了非HD和导入的密钥，这些密钥存储在一个需要密码的加密钱包文件中。正是这个文件被他改了密码，导致对应的比特币无法访问。

绝望之下，cprkrn将自己大学时代的整台电脑文件全部导入Claude。令人惊讶的是，Claude不仅从海量数据中找到一个2019年12月的旧备份钱包文件，还发现了一个关键问题：他之前使用的开源比特币钱包恢复工具btcrecover在组合共享密钥和候选密码时存在配置错误，导致暴力破解始终失败。Claude修复了这个bug，并利用旧备份文件（在密码修改前生成）成功运行btcrecover，最终解密了私钥，cprkrn得以将5枚“丢失”的比特币转移到了当前钱包。

这一案例展示了AI在加密货币恢复中的独特能力。早期比特币钱包架构复杂，助记词生成的HD密钥树无法覆盖所有密钥，许多用户依赖需密码保护的本地钱包文件。传统的恢复方法要么靠暴力破解，要么靠密码字典，但成功率低且耗时。Claude并非直接“猜”出密码，而是通过分析用户全量数据发现被忽略的旧备份和配置错误——这种“数据考古+问题诊断”的组合能力，是传统工具无法实现的。

从性能角度看，btcrecover本身是一个高效的密码恢复工具，但用户长期未能正确配置。Claude的介入相当于一个智能调试器，将恢复效率提升到实用级别。相比之下，类似任务此前需要专业团队花费数月：新闻提到，2024年曾有研究人员耗时半年破解了一个20字符密码的钱包，其内含约160万美元的比特币。而这次案例中，用户仅需一次AI交互便解决了问题。

这一技术突破的影响深远。对普通用户而言，AI降低了加密货币私钥恢复的门槛，避免了因密码遗忘导致的永久性资产损失。对专业领域，它展示了LLM在非结构化数据挖掘和自动化故障诊断中的应用价值——不仅是文本生成，还能发现人类和传统工具遗漏的错误。然而，这也引发了隐私和安全担忧：将整台电脑数据交给AI意味着高度的数据暴露风险。此外，并非所有案例都有好结局——新闻中提及另一起悲剧：2025年一位用户因法院裁决，无法搜索垃圾场找回装有8000枚比特币（当时价值约7.8亿美元）的笔记本电脑，最终全部丢失。

总的来说，Claude在此次恢复中扮演了“智能辅助”角色，而非魔法般的密码破解者。它的核心创新在于将AI的上下文理解、模式识别能力与专用恢复工具整合，创造出新的解决问题流程。随着比特币价值攀升（从早期近乎零成本到如今数万美元），这类故事将激励更多用户尝试AI辅助恢复，但同时也提醒人们：密码管理的重要性与日俱增，而AI只是工具，不是万能的。

history.title: 49,000 Lake Tahoe residents could be left powerless as AI data centers inhale electricity supply — power company looking to redirect power to 12 data centers, high demand plus a regulatory limbo equals a dim situation
history.description: Around 49,000 Lake Tahoe residents are finding out in the worst way that they might become literally powerless come May 2027, as provider NV Energy is poised to redirect the spice flow elsewhere, according to an article on Fortune.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 6 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Data center power demand is straining power grids and generation worldwide, and California and North Dakota are no exception. You might be wondering, though, what happens if you live in a specific region that straddles those two states, has unorthodox geography, and gets its power from the state next door? Around 49,000 Lake Tahoe residents are finding out in the worst way that they might become literally powerless come May 2027, as provider NV Energy is poised to redirect the spice flow elsewhere, according to an article on Fortune.The quick-and-easy answer to how that could happen is "because of AI," and while that is true as a kernel, this story is far more complicated. North Nevada has become one of the fastest-growing data center hubs in the country, thanks to tax incentives, large tracts of available land, and being close to Silicon Valley. Most of the region's data center computers sit in the Tahoe Reno Industrial Center, and they're owned by the likes of Google, Microsoft, and Apple.It's not surprising, then, to hear that regional utility operator NV Energy is experiencing "unprecedented" demand, a story all too familiar most everywhere else in the world. Fortune says that 12 data center projects in the region could pull 5,900 MW at peak, all by themselves, far beyond the region's current usage. Back in 2024, datacenters reportedly drank 22% of the state's power, a slice that could increase to 35% by 2030. The projected datacenter load growth in 2024 was 25,590 GWh, over half of Nevada's net generation.Latest Videos From You may like Data center developers building private natural gas 'Shadow Grid' power plants to sidestep strained grids Trump orders Big Tech to build its own power plants to generate power for AI data centers Nvidia-backed trial shows AI data centers can flexibly adjust power use in near real time, with global implications for energy consumption This is all well and "good," but the reason this affects Lake Tahoe in particular is the fact that its local utility, Liberty Utilities, gets 75% of its power from NV Energy, and its territory sits inside the grid operator's balancing zone. There's no direct connection from the Lake to the Californian grid, a venture that would beget a new line over the Sierra, costing at least hundreds of millions of dollars and impacting the landscape. This makes Lake Tahoe effectively landlocked in electricity flow.The problem is that the situation isn't strictly new for Liberty, and has been dragging for the better part of two decades. Per the Fortune report, Liberty was supposed to have started buying power from other sellers as far back as 2009, when NV Energy sold Liberty its California assets. A temporary agreement was set in place so the situation could be resolved, but that was extended repeatedly in 2015, 2020, and 2025, a fact that Fortune says it verified via regulatory documents.Danielle Hughes, a local resident and CEO of the nonprofit Tahoe Spark, also points out that at only 49,000 strong, even if the area could get a good short-term deal on power coming from the west side, it wouldn't be able to find affordable long-term pricing competing with giants. Understandably, Spark and another local interest group want the California Public Utilities Commission to fully oversee Liberty's procurement process, except that the entity has no actual power over NV Energy.For its part, NV Energy is building out its Greenlink West 525 kV line and intends to transition Lake Tahoe to this pipe, but watts are only expected to flow on it come May 2027, making for the closest of shaves. The operator says that the transition was set "well before data center load growth was a consideration," and "not a reaction to recent developments," but at the same time, there's no telling if the schedule could slip.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsMaking matters worse, Lake Tahoe's location makes for a cross-hatch of state, regional, and local frameworks, with Fortune going as far as stating that "no single body owns the whole problem." Adding electrons to the arc, while the Lake is seen as a rich area thanks to its ski resorts and casinos, it's actually fairly modest, and the high power demand around the tourist-heavy Christmas season needs to be paid for during the rest of the year. Whichever way this goes, it's a mess and a half to sort out. Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
history.author: Bruno Ferreira
history.titleZh: 49,000名太浩湖居民可能因AI数据中心吸干电力供应而断电——电力公司拟将电力转供12个数据中心，高需求加上监管空白导致局势黯淡
history.descriptionZh:
内华达州北部因人工智能（AI）数据中心大规模扩张，正经历前所未有的电力需求激增，这一压力已传导至毗邻的加州太浩湖地区——约4.9万居民可能在2027年5月面临实质性的电力中断风险。这起事件表面上是“因为AI”，但背后是电力基础设施、跨州能源合同与地理格局交织的复杂困局。

**背景：北内华达成为AI数据中心“避风港”**  
北内华达州凭借税收优惠、广袤可用土地以及毗邻硅谷的地理优势，已快速成长为全美增长最快的AI数据中心集群之一。以塔霍雷诺工业中心为代表，谷歌、微软、苹果等科技巨头在此部署了海量计算设施。区域电力运营商NV Energy正经历“史无前例”的需求暴涨。据《财富》杂志报道，该区域12个在建数据中心项目在峰值时可独自消耗5900兆瓦（MW）电力，远超现有区域总用量。2024年，内华达州数据中心的耗电量已占全州发电量的22%，预计到2030年将升至35%；仅2024年，数据中心负载增长就达25,590吉瓦时（GWh），超过内华达净发电量的一半。

**技术核心：电力“围城”与跨州电网的物理瓶颈**  
太浩湖地区的问题根植于其独特的地理和电力供应结构。该地区所属的公用事业公司Liberty Utilities，其75%的电力长期依赖NV Energy从内华达州输送，且其供电区域完全位于NV Energy的电网平衡区内。然而，太浩湖与加州电网之间没有任何直接连接——要新建一条穿越内华达山脉的输电线，将耗资至少数亿美元，并严重影响当地景观。这使得太浩湖在电力供应上实际上被“陆地封锁”，无法从加州本土获取替代电源。  
更关键的是，这种依赖并非短期安排。根据监管文件，早在2009年NV Energy将加州资产出售给Liberty时，就应开始从其他供应商购电，但临时协议被反复延期：2015年、2020年、2025年各一次。如今，AI数据中心的电力需求正在打破这一脆弱的平衡：NV Energy面临自身客户的巨大压力，计划在2027年5月前将太浩湖地区的电力供应切换至新建的“Greenlink West”525千伏高压输电线路。但这条线路原本规划时“远在数据中心负载增长被纳入考量之前”，且能否按时投产仍是未知数。

**性能参数与对比数据**  
- 内华达州数据中心2024年用电量占全州22%，2030年预计达35%。  
- 12个数据中心项目峰值总需求5900 MW，已超过内华达多个城市的现有负荷。  
- 2024年数据中心负载增长25,590 GWh，超过内华达年净发电量（约48,000 GWh）的一半。  
- Liberty Utilities服务的太浩湖地区仅约4.9万居民，在电力议价能力上完全无法与谷歌、微软等巨头竞争——即便能从加州西侧获得短期低价电力，也难以锁定可负担的长期合同。

**技术影响与应用场景**  
这起事件折射出AI基础设施发展对区域能源体系的深层冲击。一方面，AI训练和推理需要密集的GPU运算，单个数据中心的功耗可达数百兆瓦，远超传统IT设施。另一方面，电网规划周期（通常10年以上）与数据中心建设速度（2-3年）严重脱节，导致“电力围城”现象频现——新兴数据中心枢纽周边的居民和中小企业成为首当其冲的牺牲品。太浩湖的困境凸显出，即使在同一州内，跨区域电力调配的物理限制、历史合同遗留问题以及多层级监管（州、区域、地方）的碎片化，使得“没有单一主体能够全权负责”。  
从更广的视角看，这一案例也表明，AI引发的能源需求正倒逼电力运营商加速建设高压输电走廊（如NV Energy的Greenlink项目）、探索天然气“影子电网”以及实时功率调节技术。然而，如何在保障科技巨头扩张的同时，不牺牲普通用户的用电稳定性，将是未来五年全球电网面临的核心挑战。对于太浩湖居民而言，他们正身处这一技术变革的最前沿——一个依赖“临时协议”维持近二十年的电力孤岛，最终在AI洪流中命悬一线。
